Based on this, the connection of opposites in exile is emphasized even more: although during the time of exile the elevation of Israel is even greater than during the time of the *Beis Hamikdash*—since in this exile the revelations ascend to their source, where the source of Israel is—nevertheless, not only is the superiority of Israel not recognizable below, but they receive their sustenance through the ministers of the nations, who are in exile among them. It can be said that this is one of the reasons that exile is compared to pregnancy, for in pregnancy in the mother's womb, it is a thing and its opposite. For although "a candle is lit above his head, etc., and they teach him the entire Torah, etc." (which is specifically while he is in his mother's womb, unlike when he is born), nevertheless, while he is in his mother's womb, his head is between his knees, so that the superiority of the head over the feet is not recognizable, and furthermore, his head is between his knees, and even his food (that he eats from what his mother eats) is not through the mouth but through the navel, as explained at length in the details of this matter in *Torah Or* by our Rebbe the Alter Rebbe and in the *droschim* of our Rebbes, the *Nesi'im*, that followed.

5) Based on this, one can explain what the Sages said: "When the heathens entered the *Heichal*, they saw the *Keruvim* intertwined with one another." Seemingly, this is puzzling: the *Keruvim* being intertwined with one another indicates the greatness of the affection of the Holy One, blessed be He, and Israel, like the affection of a man and a woman, and when the heathens entered the *Heichal*, it was seemingly the opposite of affection. But the matter is that specifically then there was a most elevated light, a light from above *Hishtalshelus*, and it is just that this light was in its place (above *Hishtalshelus*) and did not shine below (in *Hishtalshelus*). One should add to this based on the saying of the Sages, "A man is obligated to visit his wife when he goes out on a journey," for since during the time of exile the *Knesses Yisrael* is like a woman whose husband has gone to a distant land, therefore, before the departure of the husband (the Holy One, blessed be He) on a journey, there was the visitation, and therefore the *Keruvim* were then intertwined like a man and his wife. Based on this, one can understand more deeply why exile is compared to pregnancy (and the redemption to birth): just as below, when a man visits his wife, the creation of the child is effected, only that at first it is in pregnancy in his mother's womb (in total concealment, and afterwards in the manner of his head between his knees, etc.), and throughout the entire time of pregnancy he grows until the time of birth arrives, when he goes out to the air of the world (in revelation), so too is it above: from the visitation that occurred at the moment before the exile, the drawing of the light (the creation of the child) was immediately effected below as well, only that its drawing is in concealment, in the manner of exile (pregnancy), and during the time of exile he grows from day to day until very soon the birth and the revelation will be in the future redemption.

6) The maamar continues that the fact that "on that night, the sleep of the King wandered"—the King of the world (the essence of the miracle of Purim)—was effected through the *mesiras nefesh* of Israel throughout the entire year. The concept is that the matter of *mesiras nefesh* is from the point of the soul that is above the individual powers, the aspect of *yechidah*. This means that the matter of *mesiras nefesh* (that he does not take into account the calculations of intellect and other powers) is that it elevates his powers to their source in the soul, analogous to the elevation of the powers to their source during sleep. However, in sleep, the elevation of the powers to the soul is in a manner where the powers withdraw from the limbs of the body and ascend to the soul as it is in concealment (in its essence), whereas in *mesiras nefesh*, on the contrary, the concealment of the soul is in arousal and in revelation, and this acts upon the powers as they are found in their place in the limbs of the body so that they should be *batel* to the soul (that he should not take them into account, as mentioned). This is what "on that night, the sleep of the King wandered"—the King of the world—was effected through the *mesiras nefesh* of Israel: because the point of the soul (which is above the *Hishtalshelus* in man) shone in revelation, through this it was effected also above, that the essential light from above *Hishtalshelus* (in which the aspects of *Hishtalshelus* ascend during sleep and exile) shone in revelation.

This can be understood in more detail based on the known fact that the completeness of *mesiras nefesh* is when the *mesiras nefesh* (which is from the *yechidah*) is drawn and acts also in the individual powers, "with all your heart and with all your soul," so that even the individual *avodah* of each and every power should be in a manner of *mesiras nefesh*, above measurement and limitation. And the *mesiras nefesh* was among all Israel, "from young to old, children and women," which can be interpreted to mean that even the individual *avodah* of each one (young, old, children, and women) was permeated with the movement of *mesiras nefesh*. It can be said that this is another matter in what is written in the maamar that their *mesiras nefesh* was throughout the entire year, for throughout the year there are all the changes of the seasons [as explained in several *droschim* regarding the superiority of the *esrog* (for the *esrog* is the aspect of *Malchus*, the matter of Esther) that it dwells on its tree from year to year], and in every season there is a special *avodah*, and even in every day (in that season itself) there is a special *avodah* belonging to this day, as the saying goes, "Every single day does its work." And the fact that they stood in *mesiras nefesh* the entire year, perhaps there is another intention in this: that even the matters of the year (the individual *avodah* of every single day) were in a manner of *mesiras nefesh*, above measurement and limitation. This is why the matter of "the sleep of the King of the world wandered" was effected specifically because they stood in *mesiras nefesh* the entire year, for because their *mesiras nefesh* shone in revelation also in the individual powers (the entire year), through this it was effected also above, that the light from above *Hishtalshelus*, "the sleep of the King wandered," was drawn in the aspect of internality, and even "he said to bring the book of records, the chronicles," that it was drawn also into the book of records, the chronicles, the root of time (as explained in the maamar), and even "and it was found written, etc." also in the book of records of Shushan the capital, literally, also during the time of exile, "they were exiled to Elam (Shushan), the *Shechinah* is with them."

7) And just as it was in the exile of Elam, so too is it in every exile, that wherever Israel was exiled, the *Shechinah* is with them, and "they were exiled to Edom, the *Shechinah* is with them." For through Israel standing in *mesiras nefesh*, that they are not ashamed before those who mock—which is connected with the movement of *mesiras nefesh*—and the movement of *mesiras nefesh* of "do not be ashamed before those who mock" is drawn to him in the individual matters of Torah and mitzvos throughout the entire day, for this is why this matter ("do not be ashamed before those who mock") is the beginning of all four parts of the *Shulchan Aruch*, and the movement of *mesiras nefesh* brings him also to an addition in the study of Torah and in the beautification of the performance of mitzvos, until the addition is above measurement because it comes from *mesiras nefesh*, and until it is recognizable in his Torah study and performance of mitzvos that they are done with *mesiras nefesh*, and certainly with *dichilu* and *rechimu* (fear and love), that in the study of Torah he remembers the Giver of the Torah, and his performance of mitzvos is permeated with the feeling of the One who commands the mitzvos, "Who has sanctified us with His mitzvos and commanded us," as explained at length in the *droschim* of the Mitteler Rebbe that in all mitzvos there must be the general intention (which is the same in all mitzvos) that he is fulfilling the command of the King of the world. And through the *avodah* of *mesiras nefesh*, and especially that the *mesiras nefesh* is drawn and acts in the individual matters of Torah and mitzvos, through this "the sleep of the King wandered," that the light from above *Hishtalshelus* is drawn even into the book of records, the chronicles, the root of time and time itself, and even "and they shall be read before the King," also before King Achashverosh literally in this physical world when "we are still servants of Achashverosh," that even when we are still in the time of exile and "the law of the kingdom is law" (and Mordechai is only the viceroy), even then "and kings shall be your foster fathers," and they decree upon those who were initially opposing the Jews and Judaism that they should assist the Jews and Judaism, until "the darkness is transformed into light and the bitter into sweet," analogous to what it was in the days of Mordechai and Esther, that King Achashverosh decreed upon the opponent that he should go and proclaim before Mordechai the Jew, "Thus shall be done to the man whom the King desires to honor," the King of the world and also—to distinguish—King Achashverosh literally. And until "the Jews had light and gladness and joy and honor," and so shall it be for us: this "light" is Torah [and in Torah itself, *orah* (light) is a feminine noun that refers to the Oral Torah, Torah in its interpretation, from which light goes out to the entire world], and "gladness" is *Yom Tov*, and "joy" is *milah*, and "honor" is *tefillin*, for the entire Torah is compared to *tefillin*. It can be said that it includes also the *tefillin* of the Master of the world, in which is written, "And who is like Your people Israel, one nation on earth," meaning that the superiority of Israel is recognizable in revelation, "the sleep of the King wandered."

8) And "connecting redemption to redemption," that from this redemption of "the Jews had light and gladness and joy and honor" at a time when they are still in the final moments of exile, they will come immediately in close succession to the true and complete redemption, that "kings shall be your foster fathers" will bring the children of Israel as "the children of Israel bring the offering in a clean vessel" to the Holy Land, the land "upon which the eyes of Hashem your G-d are always, from the beginning of the year to the end of the year," and there in Jerusalem, "the city of the great King," David the King Messiah, and there in the Holy Mountain, and in the "Sanctuary, O Hashem, which Your hands have established." And all this will be in a manner of alacrity, and even to the ultimate alacrity above all measurement and limitation, and even in a manner of "until he does not know," that one dances in with great joy that is above all measurement and limitation into the true and complete redemption, speedily in our days, truly. That all the Jews throughout all the generations, and at their head the *Nasi* of our generation, etc., and Mordechai the Jew and Queen Esther, all will be redeemed from exile—the completeness of the redemption, together with the completeness of the people, the completeness of the Torah, and the completeness of the Land, and in a manner of "Hashem your G-d will expand your border," also the land of the Kenite, the Kenizzite, and the Kadmonite, and "no verse loses its literal meaning," and on the contrary, the world of *peshat* (literal meaning), which is this lower world and the world of action, is the essence and the foundation upon which the entire *Seder Hishtalshelus* stands, as the known interpretation of "and behold, Hashem stands upon it."

In simple terms—that very soon the true and complete redemption of all Israel throughout all the generations will take place, souls in bodies, through our righteous Messiah, speedily in our days, truly.

## B's"d. Shabbos Parshas Pekudei 5730

### B's"d. Shabbos Parshas Pekudei 5730

"These are the accounts of the Mishkan, the Mishkan of the Testimony, which were counted according to the word of Moshe...". It is well known that we must analyze why the word "Mishkan" is repeated twice. Furthermore, we must understand why the first Mishkan is referred to simply as "Mishkan," while the second is called "Mishkan of the Testimony" (*Mishkan HaEdus*). We must also understand the phrase "which were counted according to the word of Moshe," as there are two ways to interpret this. One interpretation is that it refers to the phrase immediately preceding it—the "Mishkan of the Testimony". This is why the singular verb *pukad* ("was counted") is used, because only one Mishkan was counted according to the word of Moshe: the level of the "Mishkan of the Testimony." Another interpretation is that "which were counted according to the word of Moshe" refers to the Mishkan in its entirety, including the level of the simple "Mishkan." In fact, the simple, literal meaning (*pashut*)—for "no verse departs from its literal meaning"—is that "which were counted according to the word of Moshe" refers to "these are the accounts of the Mishkan" (meaning, the verse first mentions the accounts in general, and then clarifies that this was done according to the word of Moshe). We must understand the connection between these two interpretations.

2) The core explanation for this is as stated in *Likutei Torah*: the concept of the Mishkan is the indwelling of the *Shechinah*, as it is written, "And they shall make Me a sanctuary, and I will dwell (*v'shachanti*) within them." The two aspects of the Mishkan [the simple "Mishkan" and the "Mishkan of the Testimony"] correspond to two levels of the *Shechinah*: *Shechinta Tata'ah* (the Lower *Shechinah*) and *Shechinta Ila'ah* (the Higher *Shechinah*). Based on the well-known principle that every individual Jew is a sanctuary and a temple for Hashem [as our Sages said regarding the verse, "within them"—it does not say "within it," but "within them," within each and every Jew], it is understood that these two aspects of the Mishkan also exist within the Mishkan of every Jew. According to the teaching of our Sages regarding the verse "Mishkan of the Testimony"—that it is a testimony to all who come into the world that the Holy One, blessed be He, has become reconciled with Israel—we can say that the fact that Jews are a (simple) "Mishkan" is through the *avodah* of *Torah* and *mitzvos*, while the fact that they are a "Mishkan of the Testimony" is through the *avodah* of *teshuvah*. This can be connected to the aforementioned teaching from *Likutei Torah* that the simple "Mishkan" and the "Mishkan of the Testimony" are *Shechinta Tata'ah* and *Shechinta Ila'ah*, because *Shechinta Tata'ah* is *Malchus* and *Shechinta Ila'ah* is *Binah*. Similarly, this is the distinction between the *avodah* of *Torah* and *mitzvos* and the *avodah* of *teshuvah*: the fulfillment of *mitzvos*—the "commandments of the King"—is in *Malchus*, while *teshuvah* is in *Binah*. It can also be said that since the Mishkan (*the entirety* of the Mishkan) was an atonement for the sin of the Golden Calf, the simple "Mishkan" also includes the *avodah* of *teshuvah*. The difference between the simple "Mishkan" and the "Mishkan of the Testimony" is that the simple "Mishkan" is *teshuvah tata'ah* (lower *teshuvah*) and the "Mishkan of the Testimony" is *teshuvah ila'ah* (higher *teshuvah*). This aligns even better with the *Likutei Torah* that the simple "Mishkan" and "Mishkan of the Testimony" are *Shechinta Tata'ah* (*Malchus*) and *Shechinta Ila'ah* (*Binah*), because *teshuvah tata'ah* is the return of the lower *Hei* (of the Tetragrammaton, which is *Malchus*) and *teshuvah ila'ah* is the return of the higher *Hei* (which is *Binah*). According to the opinion that the command "And they shall make Me a sanctuary" and the donations for the Mishkan occurred before the sin of the Golden Calf—at which time all Jews were at the level of *tzaddikim*—it can be said that the two aspects of the Mishkan are two levels in the *avodah* of *tzaddikim*. As explained in *Likutei Torah*, the fact that Jews are a simple "Mishkan" is through the fulfillment of positive *mitzvos*, and the fact that they are a "Mishkan of the Testimony" is through the fulfillment of negative *mitzvos*. Perhaps it can be said that these two aspects of the Mishkan in the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* also exist within the *avodah* of the internal faculties: the simple "Mishkan" is the *avodah* of the heart, and the "Mishkan of the Testimony" is the *avodah* of the mind. This can be connected to the *Likutei Torah* that the two aspects of the Mishkan are positive and negative *mitzvos*, because positive *mitzvos* are in *Vav-Hei*—the *midos* (*Vav* is *Ze'er Anpin*, the final *Hei* is *Malchus*), and negative *mitzvos* are in *Yud-Hei*—the *mochin* (*Yud* is *Chochma*, *Hei* is *Binah*).

3) This can be understood by prefacing what is explained in *Likutei Torah* that *Yud-Hei* are "hidden" and *Vav-Hei* are "revealed". The concept is that *Yud-Hei* are *mochin* (intellect) and *Vav-Hei* are *midos* (emotions) (as mentioned above). One of the differences between *mochin* and *midos* is that *mochin* are for oneself, while *midos* are for others (as will be explained at length below). This is mirrored above: the *midos* are for the sake of the worlds, while the *mochin* are above the worlds. Therefore, the *midos* are in a state of revelation to others—"revealed"—while the *mochin* are hidden from others—"hidden." Now, the fact that *mochin* are hidden and *midos* are revealed means that the entire category of *midos*, even the *midos* of the heart (before one reveals them to others), are considered "revealed." Conversely, the entire category of *mochin* (even after one reveals his intellectual insights to others) are considered "hidden." The point is that the fact that *midos* relate to others is not just regarding the *action* of the *midos*, but regarding the *midos* themselves. For example, the *midah* of *Chesed* (kindness) is inherently about benefiting others; without an "other," the concept of *Chesed* does not apply. Although it is explained in many places that the impossibility of *midos* without an "other" applies specifically to the *action* of the *midos*, whereas the arousal of the *midos* (and certainly the essence of the *midos*) can exist even without an "other"—like Avraham Avinu, who felt pain because he had no guests—the fact that he felt pain over his inability to bestow kindness is because the *midah* of *Chesed* was in a state of arousal (even when there was no "other"). This is only possible when there is an existence in the world (an "other") upon whom to bestow kindness. But if there were no "other" in the world to whom to bestow it, the arousal of the *midah* of *Chesed* could not exist. Furthermore, in that case, the very concept of *Chesed* (the essence of the *midah*) would not be applicable, because the concept of *Chesed* is to benefit another. To add: the very fact that Avraham Avinu felt pain that he had no one to bestow upon proves that the (actual) bestowal to another is relevant to the *midah* of *Chesed* itself. Pain exists when there is a deficiency, and Avraham felt pain because he could not bestow; this is because the concept of *Chesed* is to bestow, and when the actual bestowal is missing, there is a lack in the perfection of the *midah* of *Chesed*.

4) Just as with *midos*, where even the *midos* themselves relate to an "other," the opposite is true for *mochin*. The fact that *mochin* are for oneself and are above any relationship with an "other" is not only regarding the *mochin* themselves—that the nature of intellect is for oneself [unlike *midos*, whose nature (even the nature of the *midos* themselves) is to bestow upon another]—but also that for the *action* of the *mochin*, one does not need an "other." Even when a person is by himself (without an "other"), he can conceive intellectual insights. Although through bestowing to another, one adds to his intellect—"and from my students more than all of them"—this is specifically regarding the *addition* to the intellect. The essence of the intellect (even the actual process of intellectualization) can occur even without an "other." Even when there is no one to bestow upon, one can conceive great and deep insights. In fact, in order to arrive at an intellectual conclusion and understand it thoroughly, an "other" can sometimes be a distraction. Therefore, most sages would isolate themselves. This is what our Sages said regarding the Sanhedrin, that they would "negotiate" all night; Rashi explains: "each one to himself," because in order to arrive at the truth of a logical argument, one must contemplate by himself. And even according to the interpretation of the Rambam that each one would negotiate with his partner—this was with his partner *specifically*, and not all of them together.

5) This is why *mochin* are "hidden" and *midos* are "revealed." Since the nature of *midos* is to bestow upon another, they are a state of revelation; it is just that while they are in the heart, the revelation has not yet come into actual practice. But *mochin*, since their nature is that they are for oneself, above any relationship with an "other," therefore, even when one reveals his intellect to another, it remains in a state of concealment. There are two aspects to this: First, the intellect that is revealed to another is not the same as the intellect that one conceived by himself [as explained in many places that the bestowal of intellect to another is through the medium of the *midos*]. Thus, the essence of the intellect (as it is in itself) remains in concealment. Second, even the level of intellect that is revealed to another is not revealed because its nature is to be revealed to an "other," for the entirety of the intellect (even the level of intellect that is revealed to another) is a matter that is for oneself. Rather, he is revealing to the other the matters that are within himself (which are above revelation to an "other").

**To add:** The fact that intellect is above any relationship with an "other" does not only refer to an "other" outside the person, but also to the matters within the person himself that are "other" in relation to the intellect. Therefore, in order to arrive at an intellectual conclusion, one must *tzimtzum* (constrict) and conceal all his faculties (even the desire to understand the intellect), because since they are "other" in relation to the intellect, they distract from the understanding of the intellect (just like an "other" outside the person). Conversely, even when he understands the intellect, the apprehension is at a distance and in separation from the other matters and faculties within the person, to the point that it is possible for the apprehension not to affect him. The fact that the apprehension of intellect leads (in general) to the *midos* of the heart and even to actual practice ("Great is study, for it leads to action") is [not because the nature of the intellect is to act upon the *midos* and the action, but] because the intellect is like light, which shines everywhere. From this it is understood that in the intellect's action upon all the faculties and matters within the person, the nature of the intellect itself is not revealed. Based on this, it can be said that the fact that *mochin* are "hidden" means that they are in concealment even from the matters and faculties within the person himself.

6) This is "These are the accounts of the Mishkan, the Mishkan of the Testimony, which were counted according to the word of Moshe." The simple "Mishkan" is the *avodah* of the heart, and therefore it is called "the Mishkan" with the definite article *Hei*, because the *midos* of the heart are a state of revelation. The "Mishkan of the Testimony"—the concept of testimony is specifically about something hidden (as explained in *Likutei Torah*)—is the *avodah* of the mind, for *mochin* are in a state of concealment. [To add: within the *mochin* themselves, the main aspect of the "Mishkan of the Testimony" (that testimony is about something hidden) is in the interiority of the *mochin* that are above relationship with the *midos*.] The concept of the "Mishkan of the Testimony" is that the apprehension of *Elokus* [including the apprehension of the interiority of the *mochin*] becomes unified with the person who is apprehending, to the point that his very existence is that which is the "Mishkan of the Testimony"—a sanctuary and a dwelling for the Divine light that shines in the *mochin*, which is above the light that shines in the *midos*. This is why it is said regarding the "Mishkan of the Testimony" that it was "counted according to the word of Moshe," because for a person to be a sanctuary for this light, it is through the aspect of Moshe within every Jew. And "no verse departs from its literal meaning," for "which were counted according to the word of Moshe" refers (also) to "these are the accounts of the Mishkan" (as mentioned in section 1), because through the *avodah* of the "Mishkan of the Testimony" (*mochin*), even the *midos* (the simple "Mishkan") are in a different state [as explained above in section 5, that the *mochin* are like light that shines everywhere].

7) It is known that the superiority of the *avodah* of *teshuvah* over the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* is that the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* is in an orderly, graduated fashion, while the *avodah* of *teshuvah* is to "leap over the wall," without graduation. It can be said that the explanation for this—that *teshuvah* (specifically) is in *Binah* (as mentioned in section 2), even though in the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* there is also *avodah* that comes from the *mochin* (including the *avodah* from the interiority of the *mochin*)—is because the main superiority of *mochin* over *midos* is that the light of the *mochin* is above and in separation from the person's own existence (as mentioned in section 5). Therefore, the concept of *mochin* (*Binah*) is (primarily) in the *avodah* of *teshuvah*, the leaping and exiting from one's existence. Based on this, the two aspects of the Mishkan are the *avodah* of *tzaddikim* and the *avodah* of *teshuvah*. The *avodah* of *tzaddikim* (even the *avodah* of the *mochin*) is the simple "Mishkan," and the "Mishkan of the Testimony" is the *avodah* of *teshuvah*. The connection between the two interpretations of "which were counted according to the word of Moshe" (according to this explanation) can be said to be that the essence of Moshe is *bitul* (nullification) of existence—"And we are what?" (*v'anachnu mah*)—and therefore the main aspect of "counted according to the word of Moshe" is in the "Mishkan of the Testimony." Nevertheless, it also applies to "these are the accounts of the Mishkan," because through *teshuvah*, even the fulfillment of *Torah* and *mitzvos* is in a more elevated manner. As is the well-known interpretation of the language of our Sages, "Teshuvah and good deeds," that through *teshuvah*, the deeds (the performance of the *mitzvos*) become "good deeds" and shine.

Now, within *teshuvah* itself there are the two aspects of *midos* and *mochin* (the simple "Mishkan" and the "Mishkan of the Testimony"): *teshuvah tata'ah*, the return of the lower *Hei* (to the *Vav*), is in the *midos*, and *teshuvah ila'ah*, the return of the higher *Hei* (to the *Yud*), is in the *mochin*. The explanation for this in the *avodah* of man can be said to be that *teshuvah tata'ah* is primarily regarding the *avodah* of *Torah* and *mitzvos* ("turn from evil and do good"), meaning that the leap in *teshuvah tata'ah* is regarding his existence, and therefore it is included in the category of *midos* (existence). *Teshuvah ila'ah* is to return his soul to its source, as it was unified with Him, blessed be He, in ultimate unity before it was breathed into him, through which he exits and nullifies his existence; therefore, it is in the *mochin*, *Binah*. Based on this, the two aspects of the Mishkan are also within *teshuvah* itself: the simple "Mishkan" is *teshuvah tata'ah* and the "Mishkan of the Testimony" is *teshuvah ila'ah*. The explanation of the two interpretations of "which were counted according to the word of Moshe" can be said to be that the main aspect of "counted according to the word of Moshe" (*bitul* of existence) is in *teshuvah ila'ah*, the "Mishkan of the Testimony." Nevertheless, it also applies to "these are the accounts of the Mishkan," because through *teshuvah ila'ah*, even *teshuvah tata'ah* is in a more elevated manner.

8) Based on this, we can explain what is written in *Likutei Torah* regarding the interpretation of the verse "These are the accounts of the Mishkan": that *pekudei* (accounts) is (also) from the language of *pekidah* (visitation/appointment) and *yichud* (union), as in "a man is obligated to visit (*lifkod*) his wife". It is explained in another place regarding the concepts of "her food, her clothing, and her marital rights" (*sha'erah, kesutah, v'onatah*): *sha'erah* (food) is the *hemshech* (drawing down of light) that is clothed within a receiving vessel; *kesutah* (clothing) is the *hemshech* that is in the category of *makif* (surrounding light) which is above interiority; and *onatah* (marital rights) is the *hemshech* of the *etzem* (essence) which is above even the *makif* of clothing. To add: the superiority of *onatah* is not only regarding the *hemshech* of the above (that *onatah* is the *hemshech* of the *etzem*), but also regarding the Jews, that they receive this *hemshech* not through clothing. It can be said that this is what our Sages said: "He who says... 'I am in my garments and she is in her garments,' he must divorce her and pay her *kesubah*." The negation of "I am in my garments" is that the *hemshech* from above is without clothing, and the negation of "she is in her garments" is that the reception of the revelation is not through clothing. We must understand that this interpretation (in *Likutei Torah*) of "These are the accounts of the Mishkan" (that *pekudei* is from the language of *pekidah* and *yichud*) comes in continuation of what is explained there: that the "Mishkan of the Testimony" is the *hemshech* (through negative *mitzvos*) that is above being clothed in vessels, and the "accounts of the Mishkan" is the *hemshech* (through positive *mitzvos*) that is clothed in vessels (the level of *sha'erah*, which is below even the level of *kesutah*). Yet, it is still called "These are the *accounts* (*pekudei*) of the Mishkan," from the language of *pekidah* and *yichud*. We must also understand the connection between Rashi's interpretation—that the repetition of the word "Mishkan" twice is a hint to the Temple that was given as a pledge (*mishkon*) twice for the sins of Israel—and the interpretation in *Likutei Torah* that the repetition of "Mishkan" twice refers to extremely elevated *hemshechim* (the *hemshech* through positive *mitzvos* and the *hemshech* through negative *mitzvos*), up to the "accounts (*pekudei*) of the Mishkan," *pekudei* being from the language of *pekidah* and *yichud*.

It can be said that the explanation for this is that through the Mishkan, which was an atonement for the sin of the Golden Calf, it was revealed that the (Higher) intent in the act of the Golden Calf was, from the outset, so that through it they would reach the level of *teshuvah*. Similarly, regarding the fact that the Temple was given as a pledge twice for the sins of Israel, the intent in this is so that they would reach the level of *teshuvah*, and through this, the pledge would subsequently be returned to Israel in a more elevated manner than it was before the destruction. Now, one of the superiorities of *teshuvah* over the *avodah* of fulfilling *Torah* and *mitzvos* is that the fact that a Jew does *teshuvah* even after the separation caused by sins ("your sins have separated") is because *teshuvah* comes from the essence of the soul, which is always connected with the *Etzem* (the Essence of Hashem). And since the essence of the soul is the "essence" (also) of the revealed faculties, therefore, through *teshuvah*, even the fulfillment of *Torah* and *mitzvos* that comes from the revealed faculties of the soul is above measurement and limitation. This is the connection between the two aspects in "These are the accounts of the Mishkan": *pekudei* from the language of *pekidah* and *yichud*, and *Mishkan* from the language of *mishkon* (pledge), because through the *teshuvah* that comes about because the Temple was given as a pledge, the essence of the soul is revealed, and through this, even the fulfillment of *Torah* and *mitzvos* is above limitation, to the point of *pekudei* in the sense of *pekidah* and *yichud*—Israel and the Holy One, blessed be He, are all one.

9) It should be added that the fact that *Likutei Torah* brings proof that *yichud* is called *pekidah* from the fact that our Sages said "a man is obligated to visit (*lifkod*) his wife" is because this statement of our Sages speaks of the obligation to visit his wife when he is about to go on a journey. This hints that in addition to the concept of the "accounts of the Mishkan"—that the intent in the Temple being given as a pledge is so that through *teshuvah* there will subsequently (in the future) be a *yichud* (*pekidah*) in a more elevated manner—even at this very time (when the Temple was given as a pledge), the *yichud* existed. As is explained in another place regarding what our Sages said: "When the heathens entered the Sanctuary, they saw the Cherubim intertwined with one another." Seemingly, this is puzzling, for the Cherubim being intertwined indicates the great affection of the Holy One, blessed be He, and Israel, like the affection of a man and a woman, and when the heathens entered the Sanctuary, it was seemingly the opposite of affection. The explanation for this is that since in the time of exile, the Community of Israel is like a woman whose husband has gone to a distant country, therefore, before the departure of the husband (the Holy One, blessed be He) on the journey, the *pekidah* occurred, and that is why the Cherubim were then intertwined like a man and his wife. However, the *pekidah* that occurred then was in concealment, and in revelation there was the destruction and the exile. The intent in this is that through the *avodah* during the time of exile, and especially through the *avodah* of *teshuvah*, the *pekidah* will be in revelation with the building of the Third Temple, and then the pledge will also be returned—the Mishkan and the "Mishkan of the Testimony"—with the coming of our righteous Moshiach, truly soon.

## B's"d. Shabbos Parshas Vayakhel-Pekudei, Parshas HaChodesh, 25 Adar, 5735

"This month shall be for you the head of the months," and it is stated in the Midrash (cited in Rashi’s commentary at the beginning of Bereishis) that Rabbi Yitzchak said: The Torah should have begun only from "This month shall be for you," etc. And what is the reason it opened with "In the beginning"? Because "He declared the power of His works to His people, to give them the heritage of the nations." According to the well-known principle that even a question in the Torah is Torah—Torah of truth—it can be said that what is stated in the Midrash (Torah of truth) that the beginning of the Torah should have been "This month shall be for you" [especially since the language of the Midrash is "it *should not* have begun... *except* from 'This month shall be for you,'" where the language of "should not... except" indicates a requirement] is also true in reality: that the Torah *does* begin with "This month shall be for you." As the Rebbe (Rashab) n.e. said in his maamar beginning with the word "Zeh," that Parshas HaChodesh is the essential beginning of the Torah. However, this matter (that the Torah begins with "This month shall be for you") is in its inwardness, while in its revealed state, the beginning of the Torah is "In the beginning G-d created."

This can be connected to the well-known discussion regarding the two opinions on the time of creation: the opinion of Rabbi Eliezer that the world was created in Tishrei (25 Elul), and the opinion of Rabbi Yehoshua that the world was created in Nisan (25 Adar). The actual creation took place in Tishrei, while the thought to create the world was in Nisan. According to the principle that the thought is the inwardness (the intent and purpose) of the action that follows the thought, it follows that the actual creation in Tishrei is the externality of creation, while the inwardness of creation was in Nisan. Similarly, regarding the beginning of the Torah: the fact that the Torah begins with "In the beginning G-d created" (the actual creation in Tishrei) is in the externality, but in the inwardness, the beginning of the Torah is "This month shall be for you" (which refers to Rosh Chodesh Nisan).

This can be understood by prefacing that there are several differences between Tishrei and Nisan. Among them is that the *hemshech* (drawing down of light) in Tishrei is through *is'arusa d'letata* (arousal from below), while the *hemshech* in Nisan is from above, an *is'arusa d'le'eila* (arousal from above) on its own. This is why the blessing for rain is on Shemini Atzeres (Tishrei) and the blessing for dew is on Pesach (Nisan), because rain is a *hemshech* through *is'arusa d'letata*, while dew is something that descends from above on its own. Another difference between Nisan and Tishrei is that the *chayus* (vitality) drawn down on Rosh Hashanah (Tishrei) for the entire year is within bounds and measure (which, in general, is the vitality clothed within the inwardness of the created beings), which is the root of the conduct of nature. Nisan being "the head of the months" [the reason it is called "head of the months" is because from it, vitality is drawn to all the months, similar to a head in the literal sense, from which vitality is drawn to all the limbs of the body] relates to the drawing down of the *ohr habilti mugbal* (the infinite light) that is above the worlds, the root of the miraculous conduct that is above nature. This is why the creation of the world was in Tishrei and the Exodus from Egypt was in Nisan: because the creation of the world is the creation of a world that is measured and limited according to the laws of nature, while the Exodus from Egypt is the exit from the *meitzarim* (limitations) of the world through the revelation of the *ohr habilti mugbal* that is above the worlds. It can be said that these two matters are dependent upon one another. The *hemshech* that comes through *is'arusa d'letata* (the *avodah* of the created beings) is measured and limited according to the manner of the lower realm, and the *hemshech* that comes through *is'arusa d'le'eila* on its own is according to the manner of the higher realm. Based on this, one must understand: since the thought to create the world was in Nisan, what is the connection between the creation of the world (measurement and limitation) and Nisan (the revelation of the *ohr habilti mugbal*)?

The dispute between Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Yehoshua also concerns the time of the future redemption. Rabbi Eliezer holds that they were redeemed in Nisan and will be redeemed in Tishrei, while Rabbi Yehoshua holds that they were redeemed in Nisan and will be redeemed in Nisan. The Tzemach Tzedek explains that they follow their respective views on the manner of the redemption. Rabbi Eliezer holds that if Israel does *teshuvah*, they are redeemed, and if not, they are not redeemed; therefore, he holds that they will be redeemed in Tishrei, because then *teshuvah* is accepted with greater intensity. The fact that the redemption of the Exodus from Egypt was in Nisan and the future redemption will be in Tishrei is because they differ in their manner. The redemption of the Exodus from Egypt was through a revelation from above, and therefore it was in Nisan. The future redemption will be through *teshuvah*, the *avodah* of Israel, and therefore it will be in Tishrei. [This is also the reason why the redemption of the Exodus from Egypt (and the subsequent redemptions) was a redemption followed by exile, the aspect of "female," while the future redemption will be an eternal redemption not followed by exile, the aspect of "male," because "if the man emits first, she gives birth to a female; if the woman emits first, she gives birth to a male."] Rabbi Yehoshua holds "not for silver shall you be redeemed"—not through *teshuvah* and good deeds [for even if they do not do *teshuvah*, they are redeemed]—therefore he holds that they will be redeemed in Nisan. The fact that the future redemption will be an eternal redemption, the aspect of "male," even though the future redemption will also be through a revelation from above, is because the revelation of the future will be from a place very high, such that even when the man emits first, she gives birth to a male. As it is written, "And of Zion it shall be said, 'Man and man was born in her, and He, the Most High, shall establish her,'" for when the revelation is from a very high place—"and He, the Most High, shall establish her"—then "man and man is born," meaning that even when the man emits first, a man (male) is born.

One must understand that regarding the manner of the redemption, we rule according to Rabbi Eliezer, as the Rambam states that Israel is redeemed only through *teshuvah*. Regarding the time of the redemption, it is brought in several *derushim* that the Midrash concludes and leaves as the opinion of Rabbi Yehoshua, that they will be redeemed in Nisan. The explanation for this can be based on what is stated in Tanya, that all the revelations of the future depend on our deeds and our *avodah* during the time of exile. This matter is (simply) agreed upon by all opinions. However, according to Rabbi Yehoshua, the fact that through our deeds and *avodah* during the time of exile the revelations of the future will be drawn down is (not because of the deeds themselves, but) because in our deeds and *avodah* during the time of exile, there is the power of the revelation from above (as will be explained in section 5). Based on this, one can explain the reconciliation of the two rulings mentioned above—that Israel is redeemed only through *teshuvah* and that they will be redeemed in Nisan—because the fact that Israel does *teshuvah* at the end of their exile and they are immediately redeemed is because in the performance of *teshuvah* by Israel at the end of their exile, there is the power of the revelation from above, the matter of Nisan.

This can be understood based on what is stated in the Midrash, a parable about a king, etc., who "did not cease to love her until he called her 'my daughter,' etc., did not cease to love her until he called her 'my sister,' etc., did not cease to love her until he called her 'my mother.'" It is explained in the *derushim* that these three matters (daughter, sister, mother) correspond to the three festivals. At the Exodus from Egypt (Pesach), when Israel was in a state of "naked and bare," and the redemption was through a revelation from above, the King of Kings, the Holy One, Blessed be He, revealed Himself to them in His glory and essence and redeemed them; they were called by the name "my daughter," for a daughter is the aspect of a receiver, who has nothing of her own and only receives what is given to her from above. At the Giving of the Torah (Shavuos), they are called "my sister," for through the Torah, they become brothers and friends to the Holy One, Blessed be He. In general, it is that through the Torah, Israel becomes one with the Holy One, Blessed be He [as is known in the explanation of the statement "Israel, the Torah, and the Holy One, Blessed be He, are all one"—that through Israel being one with the Torah, they thereby become one with the Holy One, Blessed be He]. And through *teshuvah* (which is above Torah), they are called "my mother," for they influence, as it were, the Holy One, Blessed be He. As it is written, "Go forth and see, O daughters of Zion, King Solomon, with the crown with which his mother crowned him on the day of his wedding," and it is stated in the Mishnah that the day of his wedding is Yom Kippur, for through the *teshuvah* of Yom Kippur (for on Yom Kippur is the perfection of *teshuvah*), the Community of Israel crowns a crown for the Holy One, Blessed be He. And since the revelation of the *teshuvah* and atonement of Yom Kippur is on the festival of Sukkos, therefore the essential matter of "mother" is on Sukkos. It is explained in the *derushim* that the fact that the future redemption will be in Nisan, even though Nisan is the aspect of "daughter" (lower than "sister" and "mother"), is because the superiority of "sister" and "mother" is connected with the existence of the person performing the *avodah* (for through his *avodah* he becomes a brother to the Holy One, Blessed be He, and furthermore, he influences, as it were, the Holy One, Blessed be He). Therefore, through the *avodah* of "sister" and "mother," he reaches (and becomes a brother and mother) only to the level of the light that belongs to the *hishtalshelus* (the chain of worlds) (the existence of the person). But the superiority of "daughter" (who has nothing of her own and only receives from above) is in the matter of *bitul*. And through this, he reaches the *Atzmus* (Essence) of the *Ein Sof*. This is why they will be redeemed in Nisan, for in the future redemption there will be a revelation of the *Atzmus* of the *Ein Sof*, and this revelation is through *bitul* [for this is what is written regarding Mashiach: "poor and riding on a donkey," poor specifically], the aspect of "daughter."

It can be said that one of the reasons why the revelations of the future depend specifically on our deeds and *avodah* during the time of exile is because the matter of *bitul* is primarily in the *avodah* during the time of exile. As explained elsewhere, in the *avodah* during the time of the Beis HaMikdash, since there was then a perception of *Elokus* and when they went up for the festivals there was even a seeing of *Elokus*, the existence of the person who perceives and sees was also felt. In the time of exile, and especially in the generation of the footsteps of Mashiach, the *avodah* is in *bitul*, not from the perspective of the person's existence, but only from the perspective of *Elokus*. It can be said that the power that exists in these generations to withstand all the trials that did not exist (as much) in previous generations, including the trial of "not being ashamed before those who mock," which is a very great trial, is because when a person stands in a trial by strengthening his own powers—since his powers are in measurement and limitation—there are things he cannot overcome. Not so in the generation of the footsteps of Mashiach, where the *avodah* is in the aspect of "daughter," not from the powers he has, but from his *bitul* to *Elokus*; it is understood that regarding *Elokus*, there are no limitations. It can be said that this matter (the superiority of the aspect of "daughter" during the time of exile) is hinted at in what is explained in the *derushim* (cited above) that the three matters of daughter, sister, and mother correspond to the three festivals. The order of these three matters, where "daughter" is lower than "sister" and "mother," is as they correspond to the three festivals, the time of the Beis HaMikdash. But the aspect of "daughter" during the time of exile, which is the *bitul* that comes from the essence of the *neshamah*, is not included in these three matters and is above them.

Based on this, one can explain the reconciliation of the two rulings regarding the redemption: that Israel is redeemed only through *teshuvah* and that they will be redeemed in Nisan. For the fact that Israel does *teshuvah* at the end of their exile [in exile itself, the main concealment and hiding of the exile is at its end] is in a manner where it is felt in them that the fact that they do *teshuvah* is not from the perspective of the self-image of the person doing *teshuvah*, but because "the Torah promised" that at the end of the exile they will do *teshuvah*. The aspect of Nisan, "daughter," who has nothing of her own and receives from above. And even though the *teshuvah* is by his will and choice, and as the Rambam phrases it, that "Israel will eventually do *teshuvah*," the action and *avodah* of Israel [and not like the awakening of *teshuvah* that comes through proclamations from above], nevertheless, the performance of *teshuvah* by Israel at the end of the exile is not from the perspective of their self-image, but because the promise of the Torah acts upon them to do *teshuvah*.

It can be said that this is why Israel does *teshuvah* "and they are immediately redeemed," for when the performance of *teshuvah* is from the perspective of the person himself (who is in such a state and self-image that he is capable of *teshuvah*), even though *teshuvah* in general is in a manner of a leap, nevertheless, it has some order and gradation. Since the person is limited, even in his leap there is order. That at first he comes to *teshuvah tata'ah* (lower *teshuvah*) and afterwards to *teshuvah ila'ah* (higher *teshuvah*), until he is completely redeemed from the state (exile) he was in before the *teshuvah*. And the fact that Israel does *teshuvah* at the end of their exile "and they are immediately redeemed" is because this performance of *teshuvah* is in the manner of "daughter" (due to the promise of the Torah), for the *bitul* of "daughter" reaches the *Atzmus* of the *Ein Sof* that is above *hishtalshelus* (as mentioned in section 5), and therefore, the *teshuvah* of "daughter" is above order and gradation (also above the order of a leap).

The fact that the promise of the Torah acts upon the person to do *teshuvah* on his own is the connection of Nisan and Tishrei. The fact that the *teshuvah* (at the end of the exile) is that Israel does *teshuvah* by their own power, from below to above [and not like the Exodus from Egypt, where the desire of Israel to leave the impurity of Egypt and cleave to Him, blessed be He, was from the revelation from above, which is why their exit from Egypt was in the manner of "for the people fled"], is the matter and superiority of Tishrei. And the fact that the performance of *teshuvah* is from the perspective of the promise of the Torah, which is why it is in the manner of "they are immediately redeemed," above order and gradation, is the superiority of Nisan. As explained above (section 2), the superiority of Nisan over Tishrei is that in Nisan there is the drawing down of the *ohr habilti mugbal*. It can be said that the reason they will be redeemed specifically in Nisan, even though in the *teshuvah* at the end of the exile (through which the redemption will come) there are both matters and superiorities—of Nisan and of Tishrei—is because the power for the connection of Tishrei and Nisan in the *avodah* of man is because the ultimate intent of the creation of Tishrei ("In the beginning G-d created"), when the world was created in measurement and limitation, is so that through the *avodah* of Torah and *mitzvos*, there will be in it the revelation of Nisan ("This month shall be for you"). This is why the thought to create the world was in Nisan, for the thought is the intent and purpose of the action that follows the thought (as mentioned in section 2), for the intent and purpose of the creation of the world of Tishrei (measurement and limitation) is that there should be in it the revelation of Nisan that is above limitation. However, in order for the intent to be completed—that even in the limit there should be a revelation of the *bilti mugbal*—the actual creation was in a manner where, in the revelation, the intent was not felt. Through this, a limited world was created. And through our deeds and *avodah*, and especially during the time of exile, it is revealed that the *inwardness* (the intent and purpose) of the limit created in Tishrei is that there should be in it the revelation of the *bilti mugbal*. This is why the performance of *teshuvah* at the end of the exile (the performance and action of the man created in Tishrei, limit) is in the manner of *bilti mugbal* (Nisan), for through our deeds and *avodah* it is revealed that the inward matter of the limit created in Tishrei is the *bilti mugbal* of Nisan. This is why they will be redeemed in Nisan, for then it will be revealed that even the limit of Tishrei, its inwardness, is the *bilti mugbal* of Nisan.

This is what Rabbi Yitzchak said: "The Torah should have begun only from 'This month shall be for you,'" for this matter is also true in reality (as mentioned at the beginning of the maamar), for the fact that the Torah begins with "In the beginning G-d created," even though the matter of the Torah is the drawing down and revelation of the *Ein Sof* *bilti mugbal* that is above connection to the worlds, is because the intent of the Torah is to draw down the revelation of the *Ein Sof* *bilti mugbal* into the limit of the worlds. Therefore, the Torah also contains the narrative of the work of creation in order to draw down the *bilti mugbal* of the Torah also into the world and make of it a dwelling place for Him, blessed be He. However, in order for the revelation of the *bilti mugbal* of the Torah to be also in the world as it is in reality (outside of the Torah, as it were), therefore He *opened* with "In the beginning," that the narrative of the work of creation (and similarly the subsequent *parshiyos*) is before the *parshah* of "HaChodesh," the first *mitzvah* that Israel was commanded, so that through the study of Torah and the performance of *mitzvos*, there will be a revelation of the *bilti mugbal* of the Torah also in the part of the world as it was before the performance of Torah and *mitzvos*. And since the fact that He opened with "In the beginning" is so that the revelation of the *bilti mugbal* of Torah and *mitzvos* ("This month shall be for you") will be also in the matter of "In the beginning" as it is before the *parshah* of "HaChodesh," it follows that in the inwardness of the matters, the beginning is "This month shall be for you," for the inwardness of "In the beginning" (before the *parshah* of "HaChodesh") is "This month shall be for you."

It is known that the creation of the world on the 25th of Elul was in the aspect of "Koh," and on the day of the creation of the first man (the 1st of Tishrei), there was a revelation of *Elokus* in the world, the aspect of "Zeh." However, the revelation that was through the first man is incomparable to the revelation of the Giving of the Torah. Based on this, it can be said that the fact that "This month shall be for you" is the inwardness of "In the beginning G-d created" is higher than the fact that the *teshuvah* of man is in the manner of *bilti mugbal* (as in section 7 above). For the connection of *bilti mugbal* and limit through the *teshuvah* of man being above limitation is the revelation of the *bilti mugbal* of Torah and *mitzvos* in the world as it is in the level of "Zeh," and the fact that "This month shall be for you" is the inwardness of "In the beginning G-d created" is the revelation of the *bilti mugbal* of Torah and *mitzvos* in the world created on the 25th of Elul as it is in the level of "Koh." It can be said that this revelation will be primarily in the redemption after the *teshuvah* of the end of the time of exile. For through the revelation of the *bilti mugbal* in the world as it is in the level of "Zeh" (man) through the *teshuvah* at the end of the time of exile, there will be afterwards (immediately) the revelation of the *bilti mugbal* also in the world as it is in the level of "Koh," and through this, the intent of "a dwelling place in the lower worlds" will be completed in the lowest world, below which there is no lower. And this matter is also included in the fact that they will be redeemed in Nisan [for "they will be redeemed" refers to the redemption after the *teshuvah*], that also the revelation in the world that will be in the redemption (through the performance of *teshuvah* by Israel beforehand) will be in the aspect of Nisan, the light of the *bilti mugbal* that is above connection to the worlds. And the revelation will be in a manner that this will be the inwardness of the world itself, in the true and complete redemption through Mashiach Tzidkeinu, very soon.

## Motzaei Shabbos Parshas Vayakhel-Pekudei, Parshas HaChodesh, 25 Adar 5739

"This month shall be for you the beginning of the months; it shall be the first of the months of the year for you." The Rebbe Rayatz brings in his maamar on this subject (from the beginning of his arrival in the United States) that it is stated in the Targum Yonasan on the verse "In the month of the Eisanim... it is the seventh month" (which refers to the month of Tishrei), that "the ancients called it the first month, and now it is the seventh month." Before the Giving of the Torah, Tishrei was called the first month because the world was created in it. But now, after the Exodus from Egypt and the Giving of the Torah, Nissan is the first month and Tishrei is the seventh. He raises the point: since Tishrei was initially the first month, why was it changed so that Nissan would be the first? And if it is because of the redemption that occurred in Nissan, this itself requires explanation, for the beginning of the redemption was in Tishrei—on Rosh Hashanah (the first of Tishrei) the labor of our forefathers ceased. Why, then, is the month of the (complete) redemption specifically Nissan?

2) **It should be noted** that in many discourses the precision regarding Nissan being the "beginning of months" lies in the fact that the first of Tishrei is Rosh Hashanah (for the fact that the first of Tishrei is Rosh Hashanah remains true even now, after the Exodus and the Giving of the Torah). Since it is called "Rosh Hashanah" because it includes the vitality of the entire year—analogous to the head, which includes the vitality of all the limbs of the body—and is subsequently divided into twelve "heads of months" (Roshei Chadashim), and each Rosh Chodesh into thirty (or twenty-nine) days, it follows that the vitality of the month of Nissan comes from Tishrei. Yet here it says that Nissan is the "beginning of months" and the "first of the months of the year" (even for the month of Tishrei). They explain this by noting that the vitality drawn down in Tishrei for the entire year is in the category of limit and measure (which, in general, is the vitality clothed within the created beings in their interiority), which is the root of the conduct of nature. Nissan being called the "beginning" relates to the drawing down of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is *bli gevul* (limitless), which is the root of the miraculous conduct that is above nature. Based on this, they also explain why Tishrei is Rosh Hashanah and Nissan is the beginning of months: the conduct of nature, which is always uniform, is analogous to the orbit of the sun (*shanah* [year] comes from the word *mishneh* [repetition], as the same thing returns and repeats), while miraculous conduct, whose essence is renewal (for *sometimes* the Holy One, blessed be He, disrupts the conduct of nature for a period of time), is analogous to the orbit of the moon, which is renewed every month (*chodesh* [month] comes from the word *chidush* [renewal]). It can be said that the emphasis in this maamar—that before the Exodus Tishrei was the first of the months—is that before the Exodus, Tishrei was the first, and also the "head" for the drawing down of *Elokus* that is above nature [as is also understood from the fact that the beginning of the redemption of the Exodus was in Tishrei]. Why, then, was it changed now so that Nissan is the first?

3) The core of the explanation (as understood from the maamar) is that even regarding the *chadashim* (months—from the word *chidush*, renewal), which in general are the drawing down of light that is above the state of being clothed in the worlds, the months after the Exodus are on a higher level than the months that existed before. The matter is that the revelations drawn down through the *avodah* of the Patriarchs, and also the signs and wonders that occurred in Egypt (including the fact that in Tishrei the labor of our forefathers ceased), were from the category of light that pertains to the worlds (though in this light itself, the root of the signs and wonders is from the category of light that is above being clothed in the worlds). This is the meaning of the fact that these signs and wonders were in *Mitzrayim* (Egypt), from the word *meitzar* (strait/limitation), because the entirety of the light that pertains to the worlds (even that which is above being clothed) is in a state of measurement and limitation. At the Exodus, there was the revelation of the *Atzmus Ohr Ein Sof* that is above any connection to the worlds, and through this, the departure from Egypt occurred—the departure from the straits and limitations, even from the most sublime straits and limitations. From this it is understood that before the Exodus, even the *chadashim* (months) that existed then were months pertaining to *Beriah* (Creation), and the true essence of *chadashim* as *chidush* (renewal) was achieved only after the Exodus. There are the twelve months that pertain to *Beriah*, whose root is in the twelve combinations of the Name *Ad-nut*, and the twelve months that are above *Beriah*, whose root is in the twelve combinations of the Name *Havayah*. [This can be connected to what is explained in the maamar regarding the difference between "months" in general and the "months of the year": the months of the year, despite being revelations above the world (*chadashim*—renewal), nevertheless have a connection to the world (and *shanah*—the conduct of nature), whereas "months" in general are the revelations that are above connection to the worlds.] This is why before the Exodus Tishrei was the first of the months, and now Nissan is the first: because the drawing down of light that pertains to the worlds is in Tishrei, in which the world was created, and the drawing down of the *Atzmus Ohr Ein Sof* that is above connection to the worlds is in Nissan, the month of redemption.

4) It must be understood that according to this explanation, it would seemingly appear that the fact that after the Exodus Nissan is the "beginning of months" applies only to the matter of the months that are above *Beriah* (those of the twelve combinations of the Name *Havayah*, whose essence is the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* above connection to the worlds). However, the language of the Targum—"the ancients called it the first month... and now it is the seventh month"—implies that the fact that Nissan is now the first is (also) regarding the matter of the months in which Tishrei was previously the first (meaning the months of the twelve combinations of *Ad-nut*, whose essence is the revelation of *Ohr Ein Sof* that pertains to the worlds). This is also understood from the fact that the counting of the months in the Torah *in every place* is from Nissan (for Iyar is called the second month, Sivan the third month), because after the Exodus and the Giving of the Torah, Nissan is the first month regarding all matters. Furthermore, even in the portions of the Torah before *Parshas HaChodesh*, since these portions are also part of the Torah that was given after the Exodus (and the first utterance of the Giving of the Torah is "I am... who brought you out of the land of Egypt"), the counting of the months in them is not from Tishrei but from Nissan. 

Seemingly, this could be explained based on what is written in the maamar : that after it is said "This month shall be for you the beginning of the months," it adds, "it shall be the first of the months of the year for you." This means that the "months of the year" (the months belonging to the year) are the revelations drawn down into the world in the garments of nature. The addition of "it shall be the first of the months of the year for you" means that in Israel (*lachem*—for you), there will be the revelation of *Elokus* that is above nature (Nissan) even within nature (the months of the year). But it still needs to be understood: seemingly, this matter (that in Israel, even within nature, the *Elokus* that is above nature must be felt) is an explanation only for why, after the Exodus, the months of the year relate also to Nissan. But what is the reason that the counting of the months (even of the months of the year) is now only from Nissan and not from Tishrei? Since the months of the year are the revelations pertaining to *Beriah*, in which (initially) Tishrei was the first, why was it changed so that now Nissan is the first of the months of the year? And indeed, even now (even though in Israel, even within nature, the *Elokus* that is above nature must be felt), Rosh Hashanah is on the first of Tishrei.

5) It can be said that this is similar to what our Sages said: "The Torah should have begun only with 'This month shall be for you...' What is the reason it opens with 'In the beginning' (Bereishis)? Because 'He declared to His people the power of His works, to give them the heritage of the nations.'" If the nations of the world say to Israel, "You are thieves, for you conquered the lands of the seven nations," they say to them, "The entire earth belongs to the Holy One, blessed be He; He created it and gave it to whomever He saw fit. By His will He gave it to them, and by His will He took it from them and gave it to us." It is a well-known point of precision here: seemingly, the question "The Torah should have begun only with 'This month shall be for you'" remains, even though "In the beginning G-d created..." is the root of faith. It is that the matter of the Creation of the World (and likewise all matters after it until *Parshas HaChodesh*) could have been transmitted orally or written in a separate book (and not in the Torah, for since the purpose of the Torah is commandments and instructions for Israel, it should begin with the first mitzvah that Israel was commanded). And what is the answer "Because He declared the power of His works..."? Even if the matter of the Creation of the World were transmitted orally, Israel would know how to respond to the claim of the nations of the world. Also, it requires understanding: the Torah is above the world; how can it be said that in order to respond to the nations of the world there would be a change in the Torah, which begins with *Bereishis*, even though from the perspective of the Torah's essence it should begin with "This month shall be for you"? The core of the explanation is that the reason it should have begun only with "This month shall be for you" is because all the revelations that existed until the Exodus (which are discussed in the portions before *Parshas HaChodesh*) are from the light that pertains to the worlds (as mentioned in section 3), and the essence of the Torah is the drawing down and revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is above connection to the worlds. Therefore, the Torah should have begun only with "This month shall be for you," which relates to the Exodus, at which time the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* above connection to the worlds began. However, nevertheless, since the intent of the Torah is to draw down the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* into the limitation of the worlds, and in this itself—in the lowest matters—therefore the Torah also contains the story of the Creation of the World (and likewise the other portions before *Parshas HaChodesh*), in order to draw the *bli gevul* (limitless) of the Torah also into the (lowest) matters, and to make for Him, may He be blessed, a dwelling place in the lower realms. This is why it opens with *Bereishis* because "He declared to His people the power of His works, to give them the heritage of the nations." The fact that the Torah contains the story of the Creation of the World (even though what was revealed in *Beriah* is only a restricted illumination, as explained in the maamar at length regarding the reason for "I am... who brought you out of the land of Egypt" and not "who created the heavens and the earth") is because the ultimate intent of the Torah is that Israel should also clarify the "heritage of the nations" and make it "the Land of Israel," and that the clarification should be in such a way that even the nations of the world will agree to it. Therefore, Israel must answer *the nations of the world* regarding their claim. This is also one of the reasons *why it opens* with *Bereishis*, because the perfection of a "dwelling place in the lower realms" is that the lower realm, *from its own perspective*, should be a vessel for *Elokus*. This matter is achieved specifically through the clarification that is in the way of clothing —in the way of "from below to above." Therefore, the order of events in the Torah (through the study of which in the Torah a clarification is made) is in the manner of "from below to above": the story of the Creation of the World (in which the clarification of the "heritage of the nations" is included), then the story of the Patriarchs, etc., and only after that—"This month shall be for you."

6) The explanation of the matter in greater detail will be understood based on what is written in several discourses (regarding the question of why the Torah should have begun only with "This month shall be for you" and why it opens with *Bereishis*): it is stated in *Emek HaMelech* at the beginning that the creation and formation of the worlds was through *Tzimtzum*. The matter of *Tzimtzum* (the first *Tzimtzum*) is that the light that shone initially was completely hidden, and through this, a void and empty space were made for the existence of the worlds. The intent of the *Tzimtzum* is so that through the performance of *Torah* and *mitzvos*, there would be a revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* even within the worlds. For before the *Tzimtzum*, when the *Ohr Ein Sof* filled the space of the void, it was impossible for there to be a reality of worlds, and there was no revelation in the worlds. Through the *Tzimtzum*, it was innovated that the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* would be also within the worlds. Furthermore, through this, there would also be the revelation of the interiority of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is even above the light that shone before the *Tzimtzum*. It is understood from this that it is similar regarding the fact that "by His will He gave it to them," that the intent in this from the outset is so that there would be "by His will He took it from them and gave it to us," and through this, the "heritage of the nations" is transformed into the "Land of Israel," which is most sublime. By studying in the Torah "In the beginning G-d created," that the creation of the worlds was through the *Tzimtzum* and the concealment of the Name *Elokim* (starting from the first *Tzimtzum*), one reveals the secret of the *Tzimtzum*, that the intent of the *Tzimtzum* is for the sake of the revelation. It is similar regarding the diminution of the moon, the sin of the Tree of Knowledge, etc. [and as is known that the *Tzimtzum*, the breaking of the vessels of *Tohu*, the diminution of the moon, and the sin of the Tree of Knowledge are all connected to one another]. By studying these matters in the Torah, the inner intent within them is revealed—that it was for the sake of the revelation.

7) Based on this, it will also be understood that the fact that the Torah contains the story of the Creation of the World ("Because He declared to His people the power of His works...") is not a change, G-d forbid, in the Torah—that in order for Israel to know how to respond to the nations of the world, other matters were written in the Torah, G-d forbid, that are not Torah. For the fact that the matter of the Creation of the World is a *Tzimtzum* of the Name *Elokim* (and therefore does not pertain to the Torah, which is the revelation of the Name *Havayah*, the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is *bli gevul*) is as it is from its own perspective, before the revelation of the Exodus and the Giving of the Torah. But after the revelation of the Exodus and the Giving of the Torah ("This month shall be for you") occurred, and the matter of the Creation of the World is written in the Torah to reveal the inner intent of the *Tzimtzum* (so that through this there would be a more sublime revelation), then the revelation of the inner intent of the *Tzimtzum* is like all matters in the Torah: although they are clothed in physical things, they are Torah—the wisdom of the Holy One, blessed be He, which is above the world. And the fact that it descended and was clothed in physical things is in order to draw the *bli gevul* of the Torah also below.

8) It can be said that it is similar regarding Tishrei and Nissan. The ultimate intent in the creation of Tishrei (*Bereishis* created), when the world was created in measure and limitation, is so that through the *avodah* of *Torah* and *mitzvos*, there would be in it the revelation of the *bli gevul* of Nissan ("This month shall be for you"). This can be connected to what is explained in another place regarding the two opinions: at the time of creation —the opinion of Rabbi Eliezer that the world was created in Tishrei (25 Elul) and the opinion of Rabbi Yehoshua that the world was created in Nissan (25 Adar) —that "both these and those are the words of the living G-d," but in Nissan it arose in the thought to create, and the creation in actuality was in Tishrei (which is after Nissan). According to the known principle that the intent and purpose of an action is the thought that precedes it, it can be said that the fact that the thought to create the world was in Nissan means that the intent and purpose of the creation of the world in Tishrei (measure and limitation) is that there should be in it the revelation of Nissan, which is above limitation. However, in order for the intent to be completed—that even within limitation there would be the revelation of the *bli gevul*—the creation in actuality was in a manner where the intent was not felt in the revelation, through which a limited world was created. And through *Torah* and *mitzvos*, the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is *bli gevul* is drawn into the limited world; the beginning of this revelation was at the Exodus, "This month shall be for you."

9) Based on this, it can be explained why, after the Exodus and the Giving of the Torah, the counting of the months of the year is also from Nissan, even though the months of the year are revelations pertaining to *Beriah* and the root of their drawing down is from the light that pertains to the worlds. For from the perspective of the essence and definition of the world, it is impossible for the revelations of the months of the year to be in it, because even the months of the year are *chadashim* (from the word *chidush*—renewal), which is the opposite of the definition of the world. The fact that the revelations of the months of the year from the light that is above being clothed in the world were drawn into the world (whose vitality is from the light *that is clothed* in it) was like a preface to the revelation of the "months" in general—the light that is above connection to the worlds. [It can be said that this is what our Sages said: "When the Holy One, blessed be He, chose His world, He established in it 'heads of months' (and years)." The fact that "heads of months" (which are the opposite of the definition of the world, as mentioned above) were established in the world is because the Holy One, blessed be He, *chose* His world. It can be said that the matter of "He chose His world" is the revelation of the intent of creation. For the creation of the world was (as mentioned above) in a manner where the intent was not felt in it, and when the Holy One, blessed be He, chose His world, the intent of creation began to shine in it a little, etc.] However, before the Exodus, since all matters were then in straits and limitation, the elevation that was felt in the renewal of the months of the year was only that they were above limitation in truth, and therefore Tishrei was then the first of the months of the year. But after the revelation of the Exodus and the Giving of the Torah, it was revealed that the renewal of the months of the year (which existed previously) is a preface to the revelation of the Exodus and the Giving of the Torah, and therefore now Nissan is the first also for the months of the year, and the counting of the months is from Nissan.

10) The beginning of the revelation of the month of Nissan, which is the first also for the months of the year, is from *Shabbos Mevarchim* Rosh Chodesh Nissan, and especially when the setting of Rosh Chodesh is in the middle of the week (before Shabbos), when we read *Parshas HaChodesh* on the Shabbos before Rosh Chodesh (*Shabbos Mevarchim*). And all the more so when the setting of Rosh Chodesh Nissan is on Thursday (as it was in the year of the Exodus) —when the setting of *Shabbos Mevarchim* Nissan and *Parshas HaChodesh* is on the 25th of Adar, in which it arose in the thought to create the world. This thought is the intent and purpose of the creation in actuality: that even within the limitation of the world (as it was created in Tishrei) there would be the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is *bli gevul* (Nissan), through which the intent of a "dwelling place in the lower realms" is completed. This revelation will be primarily in the future redemption, for "in Nissan they were redeemed and in Nissan they are destined to be redeemed." 

May it be His will that even though we are still in the month of Adar, where the redemption of Purim is in a manner where "we are still servants of Ahashverosh" (analogous to the fact that on Rosh Hashanah the labor of our forefathers ceased, yet even after that they were still in the exile of Egypt), nevertheless, since we are already in the 25th of Adar, the beginning of the revelation of Nissan, it is fitting and proper, etc., that the redemption should be in the month of Adar, and when the month of Nissan arrives, we will already be in the standing and situation of the true redemption [like the well-known saying of the Rebbe Rayatz regarding what we say, "Next year in Jerusalem"—that it is the same regarding the statement of our Sages, "In Nissan they are destined to be redeemed"], and we will eat there from the sacrifices and from the Pesach offerings.

## By the Grace of Hashem. Shabbos Parshas Vayakhel-Pekudei, Parshas HaChodesh, 5747

"This month shall be for you the head of the months; it shall be the first of the months of the year."
