Since the world itself is *gevul* (finite), and *gevul* and *bli gevul* are two opposites, the drawing down of the *bli gevul* into the *gevul* of the world is possible because the drawing of the *bli gevul* is from the *Atzmus* (Essence) of the Ohr Ein Sof, which is found everywhere, even within the *gevul* (and nature) of the world.

Another explanation for the superiority of miracles clothed in nature over miracles that transcend nature is that the purpose of miracles is to reveal that He, may He be blessed, rules and controls nature. This is revealed more in miracles clothed in nature. The revelation of His rule through miracles that transcend nature is that He has the power to nullify and disrupt the laws of nature, whereas in miracles clothed in nature, it is revealed that the Ohr Ein Sof rules and controls nature itself, such that nature becomes as He desires.

4) The explanation of the difference between these two interpretations regarding the superiority of miracles clothed in nature can be understood by prefacing what is stated in the *Akeidah* that even from the conduct of nature, one can know the greatness of the Creator. In addition to the fact that through contemplation of the conduct of creation (the movement of the constellations, etc.), one arrives at the recognition that there is a Master of this palace, one also knows the greatness of the Creator through contemplation of the fact that the conduct of nature is constant without change—in the words of the verse, "they shall not cease." Even though nearly six thousand years have passed, there is no weakening in the natural conduct. [Similarly, from the fact that throughout all this time there has been no weakening in the *power* of the created beings—that the host of heaven exists as individuals and the host of earth exists as species, in a manner that they are as strong as on the day they were created.]

Through this, one knows the greatness of the Creator, that His power (from which the influence in the world comes) is *Ein Sof* and *Bli Gevul*. Although the nature of created beings comes from the Divine vitality clothed within them, and the fact that every created being has a specific nature is according to the mode of vitality within it—from which it is understood that the vitality clothed in created beings is limited vitality—this is regarding nature itself. However, the constancy in the conduct of nature, which has no change or weakening, is through the power of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that is above "clothing."

However, in order to know the greatness of the Creator through the conduct of nature, contemplation is required. When one sees a miracle—a change in nature—one sees immediately (even without contemplation) that it comes from the Ohr Ein Sof, which is not limited by the parameters of the world. There is another superiority in miracles over the conduct of nature: the power of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* revealed through the constancy in the conduct of nature (even after contemplation) is clothed in the matter of *gevul*—that the *conduct of nature* (*gevul*) has no change or cessation. Through miracles, the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* itself, which is above being clothed in *gevul*, is drawn down.

Nevertheless, for this very reason, there is a superiority in the revelation of the *Ein Sof* power revealed through the constancy of nature over the revelation of the *Ein Sof* through miracles. The matter of *bli gevul* as it is revealed through miracles—the *disruption* of the nature of the world—does not belong to the world. Through the constancy in the conduct of nature, it is revealed that even the vitality of the world (*gevul*) is connected with the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul*.

This can be explained according to what is stated in the *Hemshech* of 5666: the fact that there is constancy in the conduct of nature (even though nature comes from the Divine vitality clothed in created beings, and the vitality clothed in created beings is limited vitality, as mentioned above) is because the root of the vitality clothed in created beings is (not from the *keilim*, but) from the *Ohr HaKav*, whose root is from the Ohr Ein Sof before the *Tzimtzum*. As explained in many places, the root of the *keilim* is from the *Tzimtzum* itself, and the root of the *Kav* is from the Ohr Ein Sof before the *Tzimtzum* (though it is drawn *through* the *Tzimtzum*). As stated in *Etz Chaim* that after the *Tzimtzum*, He drew from the Ohr Ein Sof a single straight *Kav* from His *Ohr HaIgul*. It is known that the fact that the *Kav* broke through the darkness of the *Tzimtzum*—and every breaking is from a side of intensity—is because the root of its drawing is from the hidden *Tiferes*, which is above the light that shone (in revelation) before the *Tzimtzum*.

Based on this, we can explain that the superiority of the revelation of the *Ein Sof* power revealed through the constancy of nature over the revelation of the *Ein Sof* through miracles is because the light before the *Tzimtzum* is limited in the matter of *bli gevul*, and it is impossible for its revelation to be within the *gevul* of the worlds. Therefore, the revelation of the *Ohr HaSovev* [which is the revelation of the light before the *Tzimtzum*], from which the root of miracles comes, is through the disruption of nature, the nullification of the nature (*gevul*) of the world. The *Ohr HaKav*, from which the constancy in the conduct of nature comes, since its root is from the hidden *Tiferes*, includes and connects the two opposites of *bli gevul* and *gevul*. Therefore, the revelation of the *bli gevul* (eternality) from the *Ohr HaKav* is also within the *gevul* (nature).

5) Based on the above (Section 4), that even the constancy in the conduct of nature is through the drawing down of the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that is above the worlds, and the superiority of miracles is that through them the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* itself, which is above being clothed in *gevul*, is drawn down, it can be said that the two types of miracles—miracles that transcend nature and miracles clothed in nature—are two modes in the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that is above being clothed in *gevul*.

Based on this, the statement above (Section 3) that the superiority of miracles clothed in nature is that the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* is *clothed* within the *gevul* of the world itself, means that in these miracles there is also the matter and superiority of the conduct of nature. The superiority of miracles clothed in nature regarding the superiority of the miracles themselves (the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that is above being clothed) is that in them it is revealed that the fact that the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* (that is above being clothed) rules and controls nature is in a manner where even nature itself becomes as He desires (the second explanation in the aforementioned section).

Similarly, this applies to miracles that transcend nature; the superiority of these miracles is also that the miracles are in revelation, and also that the fact that the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* rules and controls nature is revealed more (in a specific detail) in miracles that transcend nature. In miracles clothed in nature, where nature remains in existence, the control of the *Ein Sof* over nature is (only) that the *mode* of the conduct of nature is as He desires. In miracles that transcend nature, where nature is nullified from its existence, the control of the *Ein Sof* over nature is in its very existence.

It can be said that the difference between the types of miracles in their root is that in miracles clothed in nature, it is the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* as it relates to and gives place to *gevul*. Therefore, relative to the revelation of this light, the existence of nature (*gevul*) exists, except that the *gevul* is according to the desire of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul*. In miracles that transcend nature, it is the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* as it is above the matter of *gevul*; therefore, relative to the revelation of this light, nature is nullified from its existence. [Similar to what is explained in other places regarding the matter of *ratzon* (will) and *sechel* (intellect) [as *ratzon* and *sechel* are a parable for *Ohr HaSovev* (the root of miracles) and *Ohr HaMemalei* (the root of nature)], that the fact that the will is drawn into the intellect and acts upon it so that there will be a reason for the will is only in the illumination of the will, but the essence of the will is not drawn into the intellect; there is no reason for the will.]

However, there is still a superiority in miracles clothed in nature, in that in these miracles specifically, the control of the *Ein Sof* over the (mode of) nature itself is revealed. It can be said that the difference between the two types of miracles is similar to the difference between the nullification of *Yichuda Ila'ah* and the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol*. The fact that relative to the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul*, nature is nullified from its existence (miracles that transcend nature) is similar to the nullification of *Yichuda Ila'ah* (nullification in existence) which comes through the contemplation that "all is considered as nothing before Him." The fact that nature remains in its existence, yet its mode of conduct is (not according to the laws and properties of nature, but) according to His desire, may He be blessed (miracles clothed in nature), is similar to the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol*, for even though it remains in its existence—to the point that "a slave prefers lawlessness"—nevertheless, its conduct in practice is not according to its own desire, but according to the desire of the Master.

6) It is known that the superiority of the nullification of *Yichuda Ila'ah* is that the nullification is in its entire existence (that there remains nothing in it or about it that is not nullified, unlike the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol*, where the nullification is only regarding action in practice). The superiority of the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol* is in the nullification itself, which is a true nullification that is not connected with the person's existence. For the nullification of *Yichuda Ila'ah*, since the nullification is through *recognizing and feeling* that "all is considered as nothing before Him," the nullification is connected with the person's existence (his recognition) and is not a true nullification. The nullification of *Kabbalas Ol*, where one accepts upon himself to fulfill the command of Hashem even when the command is *against* his desire, like a slave who is *compelled* to fulfill the desire of the master—since the essence of this nullification is not to take his existence into account—is a true nullification.

It can be said that the same applies to miracles clothed in nature: even though the nullification of nature to the *Ein Sof* in these miracles is only regarding the mode of the conduct of nature and not in its very existence, nevertheless, since from the perspective of the laws and properties of nature there is no place for the conduct of nature to be in a manner of a miracle, and the change (the miracle) that is made in the conduct of nature is because the *Ein Sof* controls nature and conducts nature according to His desire, the nullification of nature in these miracles is a true nullification.

7) Based on this, it can be said that the innovation of "I will show him wonders" that will be in the future to come—that even miracles clothed in nature will be in a manner of "I will show him wonders" (that they will see the miracle)—is also in the miracles (wonders) themselves. The fact that now nature conceals the miracle within it is because nature (even when a miracle is clothed within it) is the opposite of the miracle. As mentioned above (Section 5), the change (the miracle) that is made in nature is the opposite of the laws and properties of nature. Therefore, miracles that transcend nature are in greater revelation. The fact that in the future to come there will be (also) miracles clothed in nature in a manner of *"ar'enu* wonders" (that they will see the miracle within nature) is because in the future to come, there will be an elevation of nature, such that even nature will be a vessel for the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that will be clothed within it. Therefore, even miracles clothed in nature will be in revelation.

This is the innovation of "I will show him wonders" that will be in the future to come: it is in the seeing of the wonders and also in the wonders themselves. For miracles clothed in nature now are in a manner where even after the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that is revealed in the miracle, there remains an existence (nature) which, from the perspective of its essence, is the opposite of the revelation of the *Bli Gevul* light (except that the revelation of the *Bli Gevul* light controls it so that its conduct in practice will be according to His desire, may He be blessed). In the miracles clothed in nature that will be in the future to come, there will be both superiorities: that the revelation of the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul* will be within nature, and that there will be no existence that is the opposite of the revelation of the *Bli Gevul*, since even nature will be a vessel for the *Ein Sof Bli Gevul*.

8) This can be connected with the known fact that in the future to come, the *neshamah* will be nourished by the body. Now, the body is nourished by the *neshamah*, but in the future, the *neshamah* will be nourished by the body. Even though there will still be a physical body, it will be nourished by *Elokus* and will not need physical food, and furthermore, the body will be more elevated than the *neshamah*. The matter is that the nullification of the body to *Elokus* reaches higher than the nullification of the *neshamah*. The fact that the *neshamah* is nullified to *Elokus*, since it is spiritual, its nullification is connected with its existence. When one acts (through *avodah*) to bring about nullification in the physical body, this nullification is (not from the perspective of the existence of the body, but) from the perspective of *Elokus*. However, now, the nullification of the *neshamah* is nullification in existence, and the nullification of the body is the nullification of *yesh*. In the future to come, in the nullification of the body, there will be both superiorities: that the nullification is from the perspective of *Elokus*, and that the nullification will be in its entire existence. Therefore, in the future, the *neshamah* will be nourished by the body.

9) It can be said that this is also one of the explanations for why the revelations of the future to come depend on our deeds and our *avodah* specifically during the time of *galus*, for the true essence of nullification is primarily in the *avodah* during the time of *galus*. In the nullification that existed during the time of the Beis HaMikdash, since there was then the attainment of *Elokus* and when they went up for the pilgrimage there was also the seeing of *Elokus*, the existence of the person attaining and seeing was also felt. During the time of *galus*, and especially in the generation of the footsteps of Mashiach, the *avodah* is in the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol*, and the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol* is not from the perspective of the person's existence but from the perspective of *Elokus*.

This is what is explained in the *maamarim* of our Rebbes regarding the interpretation of the verse, "And the man Moshe was very humble, more than any man on the face of the earth," that primarily this was because of the generation of the footsteps of Mashiach, where the concealments and hiddenness grew, and yet they stand with immense strength, studying Torah and fulfilling *mitzvos* with *mesiras nefesh*. Therefore (in the words of the *maamar*), "Moshe Rabbeinu fell down before himself." The nullification of Moshe, "And what are we," is the nullification of *Yichuda Ila'ah*, and the *avodah* in the generation of the footsteps of Mashiach is in the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol* and *mesiras nefesh*, and the nullification in existence "fell down before itself" because of the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol* and *mesiras nefesh*. This is why the revelations of the future to come depend on our deeds and our *avodah* specifically during the time of *galus*, for the primary innovation in the revelations of the future to come is that then the elevation of drawing down the *Bli Gevul* light into nature and the physical body will be revealed, whose nullification is not from the perspective of their existence but from the perspective of *Elokus*, and this revelation is through the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol* and *mesiras nefesh* during the time of *galus*.

10) Now, although the miracles that occurred at the exodus from Egypt were above nature, and the matter of "I will show him wonders" [the clothing of miracles in nature and that nevertheless the miracles are in revelation] will be only in the future redemption, nevertheless, according to the known fact that the opening of the channel for all redemptions, including the future redemption, was at the redemption of the exodus from Egypt, it can be said that even at the exodus from Egypt, the matter of "I will show him wonders" existed. However, this matter was then in concealment, and in the future, it will be in revelation. This is "As in the days of your exodus from the land of Egypt, I will show him wonders," that the verse compares the wonders that will be in the future redemption to the wonders that were at the exodus from Egypt, even though the miracles and wonders in the future redemption will be from Him, may He be blessed, alone (not through His court, like the miracles that were at the exodus from Egypt), because at the exodus from Egypt, the channel was opened and the wonders of the future were also there in concealment.

The verse emphasizes "As in the days of your exodus from the land of Egypt"—*kim'ei* in the plural—that this also includes the miracle of the splitting of the Sea that occurred on the seventh day of the exodus from Egypt (as mentioned above in Section 2), in order to emphasize that at the splitting of the Sea, the channel was opened and it included in concealment also the splitting of the sea that will be in the future to come ("And Hashem will utterly destroy the tongue of the Egyptian sea..."), and furthermore—even the splitting of the river that will be in the future to come ("And He will wave His hand over the river... and strike it into seven streams"). Even though the splitting of the river is higher than the splitting of the sea—for the splitting of the sea is the connection of *Atzilus* and *Beriah, Yetzirah, Asiyah*, and the splitting of the river is the connection of the worlds (*Atzilus* and *Beriah, Yetzirah, Asiyah*) with the *Ohr Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that is above *Atzilus*—nevertheless, in the splitting of the sea that occurred at the exodus from Egypt, the splitting of the sea of the future and also the splitting of the river of the future were included in concealment.

It can be said that the connection of the two matters in "I will show him wonders"—that in the wonders that will be in the future redemption there will also be the wonder of the splitting of the sea and the splitting of the river (*kim'ei* in the plural) and that the miracles clothed in nature will be in a manner of "I will show him"—is because the fact that in the future to come nature will not conceal the miracles clothed within it is because then there will be the connection of the *Ohr Ein Sof Bli Gevul* with the *gevul* of the nature of the world, and this connection is through the splitting of the sea and the splitting of the river. The verse explains that the fact that in the future to come there will be "I will show him wonders" (regarding the two aforementioned matters) is "As in the days of your exodus from the land of Egypt," for the opening of the channel for this was at the exodus from Egypt.

11) It can be said that the fact that the verse adds (after saying "And He will wave His hand over the river...") "and He will cause them to walk in shoes," is because just as shoes in their literal sense are made of coarse leather, and in order for the coarse leather to be fit to make a shoe (a garment for the human foot), it is through softening the leather from its hardness—for the leather as it is from itself (before it is softened), being hard and coarse, cannot be bent, and specifically through softening it from its hardness, it is then possible to bend it and make it into the shape of a shoe—so too it is in spirituality. The matter of "and He will cause them to walk in shoes" is that one treads upon the nature of his body and his animal soul, through which he softens them from their hardness so that they will not be "hard as a cedar" but "soft as a reed." This is what *avodah* (service) means, from the language of "tanning hides," for the essence of *avodah* is that a person fights with his nature, as explained in *Tanya* regarding the matter of "one who serves Hashem." Through this, refinement is made (not only in the nature of the person, but) also in the nature of the world at large, for this is the matter of "serving Hashem"—*Elokim* (Hashem) has the same numerical value as *HaTeva* (nature)—that tanning and rectification is made in the nature at large which is drawn from the name *Elokim*. This is "And He will wave His hand over the river... and He will cause them to walk in shoes," that the fact that in the future to come there will be the connection of the nature of the world with the *Ohr Ein Sof Bli Gevul* that is above the worlds, for which the preparation is the splitting of the river, is through the *avodah* in the way of *Kabbalas Ol*, that one compels himself against his nature.

12) Based on all the above, we can explain what is stated in the Zohar that *ar'enu* (wonders) is in the third person, referring to Moshe, because the redemption of the exodus from Egypt was through Moshe. Based on the above that at the redemption of the exodus from Egypt the channel was opened also for the future redemption, it follows that Moshe opened the channel of the wonders that will be in the future redemption. However, nevertheless, this was with him in concealment. This is what is meant by "As in the days of your exodus from the land of Egypt, I will show him wonders"—that in the future to come, the wonders that he opened at the exodus from Egypt will be revealed to him.

It can also be said that the fact that *ar'enu* (wonders) is in the third person, referring to Moshe, is a directive to every single Jew, that in order to merit the seeing of the wonders of the future, it is through the revelation of the aspect of Moshe within him. Even though the nullification of Moshe is the nullification of *Yichuda Ila'ah* which is from the perspective of revelations, and the drawing down of the matter of "I will show him wonders" is through the nullification of *Kabbalas Ol* (as mentioned above in Section 9), nevertheless, in order for the drawing down to be in revelation (*ar'enu*), there must also be the nullification that is from the perspective of revelations (through the contemplation that "all is considered as nothing before Him," and the like). Through this, there will be "*ar'enu* wonders," that the wonders of the future will be in revelation, with the coming of Mashiach Tzidkeinu speedily and truly.

## Acharon Shel Pesach 5714

### Acharon Shel Pesach 5714

"And the spirit of Hashem shall rest upon him, a spirit of wisdom and understanding, a spirit of counsel and might, a spirit of knowledge and the fear of Hashem... and he shall judge the poor with righteousness..." Through the fact that all these matters (the spirit of Hashem, the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, etc.) rest upon him, his judgment will be a judgment of righteousness.

Seemingly, one must understand: the connection between a "judgment of righteousness" and "wisdom and understanding" applies specifically to judgment based on intellect. Since it is written regarding Moshiach that "he shall smell with the fear of Hashem"—meaning he judges through the sense of smell—what relevance does the "spirit of wisdom and understanding" have to this judgment (which is based on "smell")?

The explanation is that the "spirit of Hashem" is a general concept—the drawing down of Elokus—while the "spirit of wisdom and understanding," etc., are the particulars drawn from the general "spirit of Hashem." This is the connection between the "spirit of wisdom and understanding" and "he shall judge the poor with righteousness": when wisdom and understanding are drawn from the general "spirit of Hashem," they are on a higher level, and they relate not only to judgment based on intellect, but also to judgment based on smell—"he shall smell and judge."

2) This can be understood by prefacing what our Sages said: "The Ohr Ein Sof is above, above, until there is no end, and below, below, until there is no limit." It is a well-known point of precision regarding this: "above" and "below" (even "above" and "below" in spirituality) are limitations. How can it be said of the Ein Sof (without limitation) that it is "above" and "below"?

Seemingly, one could say that "above" and "below" refer to the worlds (not to the Ein Sof), and the meaning of "the Ohr Ein Sof is above... and below..." is that it is present (and extends) through all the levels of all the worlds—above, above, until there is no end, and below, below, until there is no limit. But if so, it should have said, "There is no place void of Him," and the like, rather than "above... and below," which divides it into two distinct matters.

The Rebbe Maharash explains that in order to understand this (that the Ohr Ein Sof is above until no end and below until no limit), one must first preface the matter of Atzilus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah. The difference between Atzilus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah is as stated in Tikkunei Zohar, in the passage *Pasach Eliyahu*: "You have fashioned many bodies, which are called bodies in relation to the garments that cover them." The Sefiros of Atzilus are "bodies," and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah (even the Sefiros of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah) are "garments" for the Sefiros of Atzilus, which clothe and cover themselves within them.

The Alter Rebbe explains in the *maamar* "Pasach Eliyahu" that the difference between bodies and garments (Atzilus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah) can be understood from the difference between a body and a garment as they exist in a human being—"From my flesh I shall see Elokah." A human body, despite being merely a garment for the soul (as it is written, "You have clothed me with skin and flesh"), is unified with the soul. The garments, however, are separate from the person (and even from the person's body).

This matter of body and garments exists also in the spiritual faculties drawn from the soul. Intellect and emotions are the "body" of the human soul, while thought, speech, and action are the "garments" of the soul. That is, the soul itself is higher even than intellect and emotions, and the intellect and emotions are merely garments for the soul; through its clothing in them, it performs specific actions (to think, to love, etc.). However, the intellect and emotions are unified with the soul, whereas thought, speech, and action are separate garments.

The explanation for this (that intellect and emotions are unified with the soul, while thought, speech, and action are separate) is that the difference between a unified garment and a separate garment is that a separate garment can be removed, whereas a unified garment (like the body) cannot be removed. This is why thought, speech, and action are garments, while intellect and emotions are "unified garments": speech and action can be removed—"a time to speak and a time to be silent from speaking"—and even thought, which is constant, can be exchanged for another thought without any exertion. In contrast, intellect and emotions, because they are unified with the soul, require exertion to change. Furthermore, there are aspects of intellect and emotions that cannot be changed (even through exertion), because they are according to the nature of one's soul.

This is why the Sefiros of Atzilus are called "bodies" and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah are "garments": the three worlds of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah are like thought, speech, and action (Beriah is thought, Yetzirah is speech, Asiyah is action), which are separate garments. In contrast, the Sefiros of Atzilus, despite being incomparable to the Ohr Ein Sof, are unified with the Ohr Ein Sof that clothes itself within them—"He and His attributes are one." Therefore, they are called "bodies," being like the body which, despite being incomparable to the soul, is nevertheless unified with the soul.

3) The explanation of this matter (that the Sefiros of Atzilus are called "bodies" and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah "garments") in greater depth can be understood by prefacing that the difference between a unified garment and a separate garment is not only whether it can be removed or not, but, fundamentally, they are different in their essence. A "unified garment" means it is not an entity unto itself. When something is an entity unto itself, even if for various reasons it cannot be removed, the very fact that it is an entity unto itself makes it separate. A "unified" garment means its entire essence is to be a garment and a vessel for that which is clothed within it.

This is the essential difference between a human body and one's garments: garments are a reality unto themselves, and therefore they cover and conceal the human body clothed within them. The body, however, has no purpose other than to be a garment and a vessel for the soul clothed within it; therefore, it does not conceal the vitality of the soul clothed within it, for in the body it is evident that it is alive.

This is also the reason why the limbs of the body obey the will of the soul automatically. When the soul wills that a limb perform an action, the will does not need to command the limbs to do so; rather, it happens in them automatically. Since the entire essence of the body is to be a vessel for the soul (as above), the soul's will acts upon the body in an automatic fashion.

The same applies to the difference between intellect/emotions and thought/speech/action. The fundamental difference between them is that thought, speech, and action are a reality unto themselves (and the clothing of intellect and emotions within them is something added to their reality), and therefore they conceal the intellect and emotions clothed within them. In contrast, the essence of intellect and emotions is that they are vessels for the soul that clothes itself within them, and it is felt within them in a revealed way that the soul acts through them (that the soul thinks through the power of intellect, acts with kindness through the power of Chesed, etc.).

Based on this, one can understand in greater depth why the Sefiros of Atzilus are called "bodies" and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah "garments": Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah (even the vessels of the Sefiros of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah) are *yesh* (a distinct existence), whereas the Sefiros of Atzilus (even the vessels) are Elokus.

4) The aforementioned difference between Atzilus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah—that Atzilus is Elokus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah are separate—is also from the perspective of the root of their formation. The formation of Atzilus is from the aspect of *Ohr* (Light), and the formation of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah is from the aspect of *Koach* (Power).

The matter is as follows: It is known that in philosophical texts, the drawing down of Elokus is called *shefa* (flow/influence)—for the term *shefa* applies both to physical things, like a flow of water, and to spiritual things, like the flow of intellect—while in Kabbalistic texts, the term *Ohr* (Light) is used. The reason the philosophers termed the drawing down of Elokus *shefa* is because the name *shefa* does not describe the thing being influenced; thus, they termed the drawing down of Elokus *shefa* so as not to describe the Blessed One with a limited depiction.

The reason the Kabbalists called the drawing down of Elokus *Ohr*—even though *Ohr* is a description—is because light is always attached to its source and, when separated, it ceases to exist (whereas *shefa* has an existence even after it is separated from its source, like a flow of water, where the water that has flowed has an existence even after it has separated from the spring; the same is true of a flow of intellect, where the intellect imparted to a student has an existence even after it has separated from the teacher). Therefore, they called the drawing down of Elokus *Ohr* to indicate that the drawing down is always attached to Him, blessed be He.

But according to this, one must understand what is written: "You have made the heavens and the earth with Your great power (*koach*)." Yet, even a power can exist for (a short) time even when separated from its source, like the power of throwing, where the power drawn into the stone and carrying it is separate from the power of the hand. Since the Divine vitality that forms and enlivens the created beings is always attached to its source, why does it say "with Your great power"?

The explanation for this is that in order for the created beings (the heavens and the earth) to be in the state of *yesh* and a separate entity, there had to be a *Tzimtzum* in the Divine Light that forms them, so that it would be in the manner of *koach*, which is distinct from its source. Even though, in truth, the power that forms the created beings is also always attached to its source (for even the created beings are not a reality unto themselves and are constantly renewed by the Divine power that forms them, and certainly the Divine power that forms them is always attached to its source), nevertheless, in relation to the created beings, it appears to be distinct from its source, and through this, they become *yesh* and a separate entity. Thus, the difference between Atzilus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah (that Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah are *yesh* and a separate entity, while Atzilus is the world of unity) is from the perspective of the root of their formation, because the formation of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah is from the aspect of *koach*, and the formation of Atzilus is from the aspect of *Ohr*.

5) It is known that in Atzilus there is the *bitul* (nullification) of *Yichuda Ila'ah* (the higher unity), the *bitul* of "as if it were nothing." This means that the nullification of the emanated beings to the Emanator is not only that they are not a reality unto themselves (like the created beings of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah, which are *yesh* and a separate entity), but furthermore, they do not occupy space in relation to the Emanator—they are "as if nothing."

This can be understood by prefacing the known difference between *Ohr* and *shefa*: *shefa* is the essence of a thing, and therefore the drawing down of *shefa* causes a change in the one who influences. *Ohr*, however, is merely an illumination that is incomparable to the Source of Light, and therefore the light does not cause a change in the Source of Light. The reason the Kabbalists called the drawing down of Elokus *Ohr* is to emphasize that the fact that He, blessed be He, forms and enlivens all the worlds does not cause a change in Him, blessed be He, for the vitality of all the worlds is merely an illumination that is incomparable to Him at all.

This matter (that the light is incomparable to the Source of Light) is felt even in the light. Since the light is attached to its source and its source is felt within it, the fact that it is incomparable and has no "grasp of space" in relation to the source is felt even in the light. Based on this, one can understand why the nullification of the emanated beings to the Emanator is (not only that they are not a reality unto themselves, but also) that they do not occupy space at all. Since the formation of Atzilus is from the aspect of *Ohr* (as in section 4), which, due to its attachment to the Source of Light, is felt within it (as above) that it does not occupy space, therefore, even in the emanated beings, there shines the revelation of the higher knowledge that the essence of the Ohr Ein Sof is the true *yesh* and everything is *ayin* (nothing) in relation to Him.

6) This is "the Ohr Ein Sof is above, above, until there is no end, and below, below, until there is no limit." "Above" and "below" are Atzilus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah, and the root of their formation is from the two matters of *Ohr* and *koach* that are in the Ein Sof, which are themselves in the likeness of "above" and "below." The matter of *koach* in the Ein Sof is the matter of the descent below—that it is within the power of the Ein Sof to extend and descend below to be in the state of a distinct *koach* to form *yesh* and a separate entity. The matter of *Ohr* within it (the feeling that all matters are "as if nothing") is the matter of the ascent above.

This is "the Ohr Ein Sof is above, above, until there is no end, and below, below, until there is no limit"—that the transcendence and perfection of the Ohr Ein Sof is in both matters. First, that it is drawn below to form and enliven the created beings of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah, and in this itself—"below, below, until there is no limit"—that it is drawn not only into Beriah and Yetzirah, but also into Asiyah, and in Asiyah itself—into this physical world, than which there is no lower. Second, that even the higher worlds, Atzilus and above Atzilus—"above, above, until there is no end"—are incomparable and "as if nothing" in relation to Him, blessed be He.

7) It should be added that what was explained above, that "above" and "below" are Atzilus and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah, is in a general sense. But in a specific sense, the matter of "above" and "below" exists both in Atzilus and in Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah. Even in Atzilus and above Atzilus, there is the matter of "below," and conversely, even in Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah, there is the matter of "above."

The matter is that the true meaning of "incomparability" (that all the worlds are incomparable to Him, blessed be He) is that Atzilus (and even above Atzilus) and Asiyah are exactly equal. This matter applies specifically in relation to the Ohr Ein Sof before the *Tzimtzum*. For in the levels after the *Tzimtzum*, since all levels are in the way of *hishtalshelus* (chain-like progression) of cause and effect, it is necessary that the cause be higher than the effect. And even in relation to the Light of the *Kav* (Line), the worlds have some value. Since the formation of the worlds from the *Kav* is through the revelation of the *Kav* (for every action is through the revelation of the actor), it is necessary that they have some value in relation to the *Kav*.

In contrast, in relation to the Ohr Ein Sof before the *Tzimtzum*, since from the perspective of the revelation of the Ohr Ein Sof it was impossible for the formation of the worlds to occur—which is why there had to be a removal of the light, through which specifically a void and a vacant space were made for the standing of the worlds—therefore, in relation to the Ohr Ein Sof before the *Tzimtzum*, the worlds are completely incomparable, in such a way that Atzilus and Asiyah are exactly equal. And since the *Ohr Sovev* (Surrounding Light) that shines in the worlds in the state of *makif* (surrounding) is at least the revelation of the Ohr Ein Sof before the *Tzimtzum*, therefore, through the *Ohr Sovev*, it is felt within them (at least in the state of *makif*) that they are incomparable.

This is why even in Atzilus and above Atzilus there is "above" and "below": the fact that they are in the order and level of cause and effect (meaning they have value, as above) is the "below" in them, and the fact that it is felt in them (through the *Ohr Sovev*) that they are incomparable and "as if nothing" is the "above" in them. And similarly, conversely, even in Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah there is the matter of "above." For the *Ohr Sovev* surrounds and encompasses all the worlds, even the worlds of Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah, and therefore (from the perspective of the *Ohr Sovev* that exists within them in a *makif* and hidden way), even in the created beings there is the *ratzo* (running) and the *bitul* in existence, which is the "above" in them.

Based on this, one can understand the double expression "above, above..." and "below, below...", for the "above" and "below" in the worlds is twofold: one—that Atzilus is "above" and Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah are "below"; and two—the *bitul* and *ratzo* ("above") that exist even in Beriah-Yetzirah-Asiyah, and the existence ("below") that exists even in Atzilus and above Atzilus.

8) It is known that the Light of the *Kav* also has a root in the Light before the *Tzimtzum*. For in the Light before the *Tzimtzum* there are (generally) two levels: as it is before it arose in His will to emanate and create, when it was above any relation to the worlds; and as it is after it arose in His will to emanate and create, through which it came into the state of relation to the worlds. These two levels are the root of the *Ohr Sovev* and the *Ohr Memaleh* (Filling Light) after the *Tzimtzum*. The *Ohr Sovev* has its root in the Light before the arising of the will, and the *Ohr Memaleh* (the Light of the *Kav*) has its root in the Light of the arising of the will. This is "the Ohr Ein Sof is above, above, until there is no end, and below, below, until there is no limit"—that both matters (of "above, above..." and "below, below...") are in the Ohr Ein Sof itself.

9) It is known that the Light (before it was revealed) was included in the essence of the Source of Light itself. However, while included in the Source of Light, it is not in the state of "light" at all, but only that it is within His ability to shine. Based on this, one must say that the two aforementioned levels in the Light that is in the state of revelation (before the *Tzimtzum*) also have a root in the Light that is included.

The point of the explanation is that the fact that the Essence has the ability to shine is not a specific matter (a source for light), but rather that He, blessed be He, is All-Powerful. From this it is understood that in the ability to shine (as it is in the Essence) there is also the ability not to shine. Both (that it is in His ability to shine and that it is in His ability not to shine) are one matter. Since the Light included in His Essence is that the Essence has the ability to shine (as above), and in this ability there is also the ability not to shine, therefore, even as the light is drawn into revelation, it contains both matters of concealment and revelation, above relation to the worlds and in relation to the worlds, which are the root of the *Sovev* and *Memaleh* after the *Tzimtzum*, from which the "above" and "below" in the worlds are made.

10) This is "And the spirit of Hashem shall rest upon him..." It is known that the innovation of Moshiach is that through him the two matters of "above" and "below" will be unified. The beginning of the connection of "above" and "below" was at the time of the splitting of the Red Sea, but then it was only for a time, and it was a preparation for the Giving of the Torah—the higher ones descended below and the lower ones ascended above. The true connection of "above" and "below" will be in the days of Moshiach.

As it is stated in *Midrash Tehillim* on the verse "Who will give from Zion...": You find it written twice in the Book of Psalms, "Who will give from Zion," one in the first book and one in the second book. The Master said one and the student said one. The Master said, "Who will give that their heart would be this for them," and the student said, "And who will give all the people of Hashem to be prophets." Neither the words of the Master nor the words of the student are fulfilled in this world, but in the World to Come both are fulfilled. From where do we know the words of the Master? As it is said, "And I will give you a new heart." From where do we know the words of the student? As it is said, "And it shall be after this that I will pour out My spirit upon all flesh."

The fact that the physical human heart will be "to fear Me... all the days" is that the "below" will be connected with the "above." And the fact that "I will pour out My spirit upon all flesh" is that the "above" will be connected with the "below." The fact that in the future the connection of "above" and "below" will exist [in both ways: that the "below" will be elevated to the "above" and that the "above" will be drawn down to the "below"] is because then both (the "above" and the "below") will be as they are from the perspective of the ability of the Essence, that it is in His ability to shine and in His ability not to shine. And since the ability to shine and the ability not to shine are one matter (as in section 9), therefore, even the "above" and "below" (when their primary root is revealed in them, as they are in the ability) will be connected and unified as one.

This is "And the spirit of Hashem shall rest upon him"—"spirit" is the Ohr Ein Sof as it is included in the essence of the Source of Light, which is the ability of the Essence. Through the revelation of the "spirit of Hashem" that will be in Moshiach, there will be unification even in the specific matters. This is why the verse continues, "a spirit of wisdom and understanding, a spirit of counsel and might, a spirit of knowledge and the fear of Hashem"—for wisdom and understanding are two opposing matters, and yet they will be unified as one [which is why they are included in one "spirit," the "spirit of wisdom and understanding"], because in the wisdom and understanding of Moshiach it will be evident in revelation that they are drawn from their root, the "spirit of Hashem" (*spirit* of wisdom and understanding), and therefore they will be unified as one. [And similarly regarding counsel and might, knowledge and fear, that despite being opposites, they will be unified as one.]

This is also the connection of the "spirit of wisdom and understanding" with "he shall judge the poor with righteousness," even though the judgment of Moshiach will be according to smell, for smell is *makif* (surrounding). For in Moshiach, the *makif* (smell) and the *pnim* (internal—wisdom and understanding) will be unified as one. This is also what the verse continues, "And the wolf shall dwell with the lamb..."—that the connection of "above" and "below" will be not only in Moshiach himself, but through Moshiach this matter will (likewise) be in the entire world, until "the wolf shall dwell with the lamb," and even in the category of the inanimate, as the verse continues, "for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of Hashem," that even the physical earth (the inanimate) will be full of the knowledge of Hashem, with the coming of Moshiach Tzidkeinu, very soon.

## With the help of Heaven. Shabbos Parshas Kedoshim, Mevarchim HaChodesh Iyar, 5714.

### With the help of Heaven. Shabbos Parshas Kedoshim, Mevarchim HaChodesh Iyar, 5714.

"The world stands on three things: on Torah, on avodah, and on gemilus chasadim". The Rebbe, my father-in-law, brings in his maamar entitled *Zeh* the interpretation of the Alter Rebbe: "The world (*olam*) stands on three things" — on three things the concealment (*he'elem*) stands. The maamar explains the connection between the Alter Rebbe’s interpretation (the concealment stands) and the simple interpretation (the world stands), for the essence of the world is concealment (*he'elem*). The maamar adds that the concealment within the world exists because the creation of the world came about through the concealment of the Ohr. As it is stated in *Etz Chaim*, when the Ohr Ein Sof filled the entire reality, there was no place for the world to exist; in order for there to be a place for the worlds, it was necessary for the Ohr to be withdrawn. It can be said that this addition further emphasizes the concept of the world’s concealment: the concealment within it is not like something added onto it, but rather that the *creation* of the world itself is through the concealment of the Ohr. Furthermore, from the perspective of the state of revelation, there was not even a place for the worlds (and not merely that they did not yet exist in actuality).

The maamar continues that the purpose of the concealment is for the sake of revelation. Through removing the concealment, the inner Ohr that is hidden within the concealment is revealed. This is the meaning of "the world stands on three things": through avodah in Torah, avodah, and gemilus chasadim, we remove the concealment and the inner Ohr is revealed. It can be said that the statement in the maamar that the purpose of the concealment is for the sake of revelation is intended to explain the Mishnah, "the *world* stands on three things." Even though through Torah, avodah, and gemilus chasadim a revelation of Elokus is drawn down, nevertheless it says "the *world* stands" (*the concealment* stands), because the purpose of the concealment is for the sake of revelation, and the purpose of any thing is its inner essence. Therefore, it says the world (the concealment) stands, because the inner essence of the world (concealment) is precisely that which allows for the revelation of Elokus to be drawn into it.

Based on this, we can explain the phrase "on *three things* the world stands," which implies that for the world to stand (for there to be a revelation of Elokus in the world), it is not enough to have the general concept of Torah and mitzvos, but specifically the concept of "three things." For the concept of the world’s standing is that the revelation of Elokus drawn into the world through Torah and mitzvos should not be like something added to it, but rather that it should be revealed that the purpose and inner essence of the world (concealment) itself is that there should be a revelation of Elokus within it. This aspect of Torah and mitzvos is achieved through their division into three things (three categories): Torah, avodah, and gemilus chasadim.

2) This can be understood by prefacing what was explained earlier (in the previous maamar) regarding the explanation of the statement of our Sages, "Ohr Ein Sof is *ma'alah ma'alah* (above and beyond) until Ein Ketz, and *lematah matah* (below and below) until Ein Tachlis." *Ma'alah* and *matah* (generally speaking) refer to Atzilus and the worlds of Biya. The meaning of "Ohr Ein Sof is *ma'alah*..." and "*lematah*..." is that the transcendence and perfection of the Ohr Ein Sof manifests in two ways: 1) In the matter of the *hemshech* (drawing down) and descent below to bring into being and sustain the created beings of Biya—and in this itself, "*lematah matah* until Ein Tachlis," meaning it is drawn down to sustain the created beings in this physical world, below which there is no lower level, even extending to sustain the kelipos. 2) In the matter of *romemus* (exaltation), that even the higher worlds—Atzilus and that which is above Atzilus—"*ma'alah ma'alah*," their existence is only from a mere *ha'arah* (illumination) that is as nothing compared to Him, may He be blessed. These two matters are (generally) the Ohr HaMemaleh and the Ohr HaSovev. The Ohr HaMemaleh is the matter of descent and drawing down below to sustain the created beings according to their capacity, and the Ohr HaSovev is the matter of wonder and exaltation, where all the worlds (and even the Ohr that is enclothed within them) are as nothing and of no account compared to Him, may He be blessed. From these two matters in the Ohr Ein Sof, the two aspects of *ma'alah* and *matah* are created in the worlds. The aspect of *ma'alah* and *matah* exists in all worlds: the reality of the worlds (even the reality in Atzilus and above Atzilus) is the *matah* within them, and the matter of *ratzo*—the departure from reality (which exists in all worlds, even in Biya)—is the *ma'alah* within them. These two aspects in the worlds derive from the two aforementioned matters in the Ohr Ein Sof. From the Ohr HaMemaleh, which is in a movement of descent and drawing down, the *matah* (reality) of the worlds is formed, and the *ma'alah* of the worlds (the *ratzo* within them, where they desire to depart from their reality) is from the revelation of the *romemus* of the Ohr Ein Sof (Sovev).

3) It is known that the fact that creation is for the sake of man refers not only to the creation of the worlds (including the world of Atzilus and the worlds of the Ein Sof above Atzilus), but to all revelations, even the most sublime. Based on this, it can be said that the intent of the two aforementioned matters in the Ohr Ein Sof—the descent and drawing down below of the Ohr HaMemaleh and the elevation and exaltation of the Ohr HaSovev, including the root of these two matters as they are in the Ohr Ein Sof before the Tzimtzum [the Ohr of *Alos HaRatzon*, which is the root of the Ohr HaMemaleh, and the Ohr that is before *Alos HaRatzon*, which is the root of the Ohr HaSovev]—is so that there would be (a parallel to) these two matters in the avodah of man. Generally, these are the two lines of Torah and tefillah. Tefillah is an elevation from below to above, for through the *hisbonenus* (contemplation) of tefillah, one departs from his reality, to the point of transcending physicality. Torah is a drawing down from above to below. The matter of engaging in Torah is (not that man nullifies his intellect, but on the contrary) that the Torah is drawn down and enclothed within the intellect of man—a drawing down from above to below. Furthermore, the *hemshech* of Torah (that it is drawn into the intellect of man) occurs even when man is in a state that is the opposite of purity, similar to the matter of "Ohr Ein Sof *lematah matah* until Ein Tachlis," which is drawn to sustain not only the created beings of this physical world, but even the kelipos. And just as in the matter of "*lematah matah* until Ein Tachlis," even when it is drawn below to sustain the created beings and even the kelipos, the Ohr does not change thereby, so too it is with the *hemshech* of Torah: even as it is drawn into the intellect of man, and even when man is in a state that is the opposite of purity, it remains the word of Hashem.

4) Now, just as the matter of "*ma'alah* until Ein Ketz" exists even in the lower worlds—as explained above (section 2), that even in the created beings of Biya, and even in the created beings of this world, the *romemus* of the Ohr Ein Sof shines, such that all the worlds are as nothing and of no account compared to Him, may He be blessed, and thereby a *ratzo* for Elokus is created within them, where they desire to depart from their reality—so too it is with the elevation of tefillah (which is a parallel to the matter of "*ma'alah* until Ein Ketz"). Even when a person is "*lematah matah* until Ein Tachlis," through tefillah he is elevated (meaning he is in a state of *ratzo* and desire to ascend) "*ma'alah ma'alah* until Ein Ketz." As it is written, "a ladder set on the ground and its top reaches the heavens," and it is stated in the Zohar that "ladder" refers to tefillah. Even when a person is at the lowest level ("on the ground"), through the ladder of tefillah he reaches and ascends to the heavens. However, because he is in a lowly state, in order to be aroused with *ratzo* and desire to ascend, he must contemplate (not only the matters of Hashem’s greatness explained in tefillah, but also) this very matter—that he is in a lowly state. This applies both regarding his thoughts, speech, and actions—that sometimes he falls (he fails) even in actual deed, and certainly in speech, and even more so in thought—and regarding his intellect and *midos*. Although the primary avodah of man is "turn from evil and do good" in actual deed, speech, and thought, this matter (that the primary thing is the actual deed) applies to the *kavanah* of the higher realms. But regarding the state of the person, since the intellect and *midos* are unified with the *nefesh*, and thought, speech, and action are only garments (as explained at length in the previous maamar), the state of his intellect and *midos* (whether they are in holiness or the opposite) is of greater concern than his thought, speech, and action. And certainly when he contemplates the essence of his *nefesh*, that even when his thought, speech, action, and even his *midos* and intellect are in holiness, it is possible that his essential *nefesh* is in the depths of the kelipos, as is known regarding what Rabbi Yochanan ben Zakkai said, "I do not know by which path they are leading me." Through contemplating all this and feeling that he is in a lowly state, he is aroused with a *ratzo* to depart from his reality and ascend above.

5) It was explained above (in the previous maamar) that the two matters in the Ohr Ein Sof (*ma'alah ma'alah* until Ein Ketz and *lematah matah* until Ein Tachlis) in their first root (in the Ohr as it is included in the *Atzmus*, which is the matter of *yecholes*—potential) are that it is within His power to shine and within His power not to shine. And since in the category of *yecholes*, both (the power to shine and the power not to shine) are one matter, therefore, even when they are drawn down in revelation and thereby become two matters, *ma'alah ma'alah* and *lematah matah*, they are included in one another. So too it is with Torah and tefillah, which are included in one another. Even in Torah, which is a drawing down from above to below, there is the matter of elevation from below to above, and likewise in tefillah, which is an elevation from below to above, there is also the matter of drawing down from above to below.

This can be understood based on what our Sages said: "The first three hours, the Holy One, blessed be He, sits and engages in Torah." The well-known precision in this is: "Whoever reads and studies, the Holy One, blessed be He, reads and studies opposite him". Since Yisrael engage in Torah throughout the entire day (this one studies at this hour, and that one studies at that hour), the Holy One, blessed be He, should also be engaging in Torah throughout the entire day. Yet they said that (specifically) in the first three hours, the Holy One, blessed be He, sits and engages in Torah. The point of the explanation is that the fact that "whoever reads and studies, the Holy One, blessed be He, reads and studies opposite him" is a *hemshech* from above that comes through *is'arusa d'lesata* (the avodah of man). Therefore, the *hemshech* is according to the measure of the *is'arusa d'lesata*. This is why they said "opposite him"—that the Holy One, blessed be He, reading and studying is "opposite him," according to the study of the person. But the fact that in the first three hours the Holy One, blessed be He, sits and engages in Torah is a *hemshech* from above on its own, from a place that *is'arusa d'lesata* cannot reach. This *hemshech* is the giving of strength to man so that he can engage in Torah, and so that through his engagement in Torah, the Holy One, blessed be He, will read and study opposite him. This is the meaning of "My teaching shall drop as the rain, My speech shall distill as the dew". The difference between dew and rain is that the descent of rain depends on the avodah of man (as it is written, "And it shall be if you listen... [and only then] I will give the rain of your land..."), while dew does not depend on the avodah of man (as our Sages said, "Dew is never withheld"). So too it is in spirituality: rain is the *hemshech* that comes through *is'arusa d'lesata*, and dew is the *hemshech* from above on its own. The reason Torah is compared to dew and rain is because Torah contains both matters. The level of Torah that is drawn down through the engagement of man—"My teaching shall drop as the rain"—and the level of Torah whose *hemshech* is from above (by way of *is'arusa d'le'eila* on its own)—"My speech shall distill as the dew." This is the connection of "the Holy One, blessed be He, sits and engages in Torah" to the first three hours, because the root of rain is in the *Zayin Tachtonos* of *Atik* [as is known regarding "sevenfold as the light of the seven days", that "sevenfold" is forty-nine, and "sevenfold as the light of the seven days"—forty-nine times seven—is the gematria of *geshem* (rain). From this it is understood that rain belongs to the seven days, which are the *Zayin Tachtonos* of *Atik*], and the root of dew is in the *Gimmel Rishonos* of *Atik*. This is the connection of "the Holy One, blessed be He, sits and engages in Torah" (the dew of Torah) to the first three hours, because the first three [hours] are the *Gimmel Rishonos* of *Atik*.

6) Now, just as dew (in the literal sense) descends and is drawn below, so too it is with the dew of Torah, which is drawn and revealed below in the Torah that man studies. For in addition to the fact that "the Holy One, blessed be He, sits and engages in Torah" (the aspect of the dew of Torah) is the giving of strength to man so that he can engage in Torah and so that through his engagement in Torah, the Holy One, blessed be He, will read and study opposite him, the matter of the dew of Torah itself is also drawn and revealed below in the Torah that man studies. In the *hemshech* of the Torah from above into the Torah that man studies, there are two matters: 1) The Torah as it is in the *Zayin Tachtonos* of *Atik*—the rain of Torah. The *hemshech* of this aspect is through the engagement of man in Torah, for since the *Zayin Tachtonos* of *Atik* are enclothed in *Arich*, the root of the *hishtalshelus*, the engagement of man in Torah is the *is'arusa d'lesata* that arouses and draws down this aspect. 2) The Torah as it is in the *Gimmel Rishonos* of *Atik*—the dew of Torah. The *hemshech* of this aspect is from above, and the engagement of man in Torah is only like a vessel in which the dew of Torah is drawn and revealed. It can be said that the study of Torah by man being a vessel for the dew of Torah applies primarily to the study of the *pnimiyus* of the Torah. Since the *pnimiyus* of the Torah (even as it is drawn below) is the "Tree of Life" that is above the matter of *birurim*, it is like a vessel for the essence of Torah that is above any connection to the *hishtalshelus* (*Gimmel Rishonos* of *Atik* that are not enclothed in *Arich*)—the dew of Torah. Based on this, one can explain the connection between the two versions in the statement of our Sages, "Whoever uses the *Ohr* of Torah, the *Ohr* of Torah sustains him." The version in the Gemara is "*Ohr* of Torah," and the version in the Yalkut is "*Tal* (dew) of Torah." It can be said that *Ohr* of Torah is the *pnimiyus* of the Torah, and the study of the *pnimiyus* of the Torah is a vessel for the *Tal* of Torah.

7) So too it is in tefillah: although generally it is an elevation from below to above, it also contains the matter of drawing down from above to below. The matter is that the beginning of the Shemoneh Esrei [which is the essence of tefillah] is with the verse, "Hashem, open my lips, and my mouth shall declare Your praise." The recitation of this verse is (not only a preface to tefillah, but) the beginning of the tefillah itself, as it is stated in the Gemara that it is like a long tefillah. In the Targum on this verse, "Hashem, open my lips," it is interpreted, "Open my lips in Torah." This means that the request in this verse at the beginning of tefillah is that the tefillah ("and my mouth shall declare Your praise") should be like Torah ("Hashem, open my lips in Torah"). Just as in Torah, the Torah that man studies is the word of Hashem—"in *my* mouth which I have put in your mouth"—and man is like one who answers after the reader, so too the tefillah should be in the manner of "and my mouth shall declare *Your* praise," that the tefillah of man should be (not *his* tefillah, but) only that through it there should be drawn (the word *yagid* comes from the root of *hemshech*) *Your* praise—the tefillah of the Holy One, blessed be He.

The explanation of this in greater detail can be understood based on what is stated in the Gemara that when the Rebbe heard that Rabbi Chiya and his sons were like the Patriarchs, the Rebbe placed them before the *teivah* (the prayer stand). He said "He causes the wind to blow and the rain to descend," and the rain came. When he reached "He sustains the living," the world trembled. The Alshich writes that the request "Hashem, open my lips, and my mouth shall declare Your praise" at the beginning of the Shemoneh Esrei is that our tefillah should be like the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons. It is not understood how it is possible to say that our tefillah should be like the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons [especially based on what is stated in the Gemara there, that Rabbi Chiya and his sons were like the Patriarchs]. Also, it must be understood that Rabbi Chiya and his sons would pray every day, yet the *hemshech* through the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons (the wind blowing, the rain coming, the world trembling) occurred specifically when the Rebbe placed them before the *teivah*. Because they prayed before the *teivah* and were *shluchei tzibbur* (communal prayer leaders), their tefillah possessed the power of the *tzibbur*, for regarding the tefillah of the many (communal tefillah), it is written, "Behold, G-d is mighty and does not despise." And even though communal tefillah exists every day, and the fact that specifically through this tefillah the wind blew, etc., was because of the greatness of Rabbi Chiya and his sons who were like the Patriarchs, nevertheless, even in the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons, for the wind to blow, etc., it was specifically when they were *shluchei tzibbur*.

Seemingly, one could explain this based on the known principle that from the fact that in every request in the Shemoneh Esrei we conclude "Blessed are You, Hashem," it is proven that even in the tefillah of an individual, there is no doubt at all that the Holy One, blessed be He, fulfills his request. However, in the tefillah of an individual, it is possible that the *hashpa'ah* (influence) is drawn to him above, or that it is drawn (also below, but only) in spirituality, and the advantage of communal tefillah (which is desired and accepted by the world) is that the *hashpa'ah* is drawn also below in physicality. Now, in communal tefillah, there are several levels of gradation, and the greater the request, and especially when the congregation praying are not worthy vessels for it, it requires a greater tefillah, and also that the *shaliach tzibbur* should be an elder (or the most distinguished of the congregation) so that his tefillah will be heard. Based on this, one could seemingly explain regarding Rabbi Chiya and his sons, that the fact that through their tefillah when they were *shluchei tzibbur* the wind blew and the rain came (in physicality) is (primarily) due to the advantage of the tefillah of the many. However, even in communal tefillah (and especially in a great request for which the congregation is not worthy), the *shaliach tzibbur* must be someone whose tefillah is heard. But this explanation is insufficient, for from the fact that they said Rabbi Chiya and his sons were like the Patriarchs, it implies that the fact that through their tefillah the wind blew and the rain came is because of the greatness of *their* level (that their tefillah is heard like the tefillah of the Patriarchs). And yet, the fact that the wind blew, etc., was specifically when they were *shluchei tzibbur*. The matter is that the *hemshech* through tefillah is in physicality [and to the point that through the *hemshech* a change is made in the created beings, to heal the sick and bless the years, unlike the *hemshech* through Torah and mitzvos, which is (primarily) above] when the *hemshech* in physicality is from the requests of the needs of the one praying [although even then there are many levels of gradation, for in addition to the difference between the tefillah of an individual and the tefillah of the many, there is also a difference in the level of the person praying (the individual, or the *shaliach tzibbur*), for there is one whose tefillah is heard more]. Since the *hemshech* of "He causes the wind to blow," etc., were not requests for their own needs, therefore, through their tefillah (when they prayed for themselves), they drew these matters down in spirituality (similar to the *hemshech* through Torah and mitzvos). But when the Rebbe placed them before the *teivah* and they became *shluchei tzibbur* of the entire Jewish people, thereby these matters were included in their requests for their needs [in addition to the fact that because they prayed as *shluchei tzibbur* of the entire Jewish people, the advantage of the power of the many was added to their tefillah], and therefore, through their tefillah then, the matters were drawn down also in physicality.

Another aspect of this is that the fact that through the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons [even when they were *shluchei tzibbur* and the matters of "He causes the wind to blow," etc., became their requests for their needs] the wind blew, etc., is because the *Rebbe*, who was the master of Torah, placed them before the *teivah*, and thereby their tefillah also contained the matter of Torah. The matter is that in the *hemshech* through tefillah, which is the avodah of the lower being, the state of the lower being is relevant, and for the *shefa* to be drawn to the lower being, it is specifically when he is (somewhat) a vessel for the *hemshech*. And the *hemshech* through Torah, since it is *from above*, is drawn down below, even when the lower being is not a vessel (at all) for the *hemshech*. Therefore, the fact that through the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons there was a *hemshech* in the world (even though the world then was not a vessel for this) is because their tefillah contained (also) the matter of Torah (by the *Rebbe* placing them before the *teivah*). And the fact that the *hemshech* was specifically through the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons (and not through the Rebbe) is because for there to be a *change* in the physical, it is through the *hemshech* of the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the *hishtalshelus*, and the *hemshech* of this Ohr is specifically through the elevation of *MAN* of tefillah. In this matter, Rabbi Chiya and his sons were greater than the Rebbe. For the advantage of tefillah, which is the elevation of *MAN* to draw down the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the *hishtalshelus*, is the *bitul* in tefillah, that the person praying is like a servant before his master, and Rabbi Chiya and his sons were like the Patriarchs, who were a chariot for Elokus, and a chariot is *bitul* in the ultimate sense. Therefore, the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons was an elevation of *MAN* to draw down an extremely sublime Ohr, through which *hemshech* a change is made in the created beings, even to the change of the resurrection of the dead. However, for the *hemshech* to be in the world (even though the world is not a vessel for this), it is through the fact that their tefillah contained (also) the matter of Torah, a *hemshech* from above.

This is the meaning of the request in the verse "Hashem, open my lips, and my mouth shall declare Your praise"—that our tefillah should be like the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons. For in the advantage of the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons, there are two matters: the advantage of the tefillah (of theirs) itself, that the elevation of *MAN* of their tefillah is much more sublime than the elevation of *MAN* of an ordinary tefillah, and that their tefillah also contained the matter of Torah. And in the request in the aforementioned verse, there are two matters: the request "and my mouth shall declare Your praise" is that his tefillah should be like one who answers after the reader, after the tefillah of the Holy One, blessed be He, and the tefillah of the Holy One, blessed be He, is no less than the tefillah of Rabbi Chiya and his sons. And the request "Hashem, open my lips in Torah" is that his tefillah should also contain the advantage of Torah, a *hemshech* from above to below.

8) Now, through the inclusion of Torah and tefillah [in Torah, which is a *hemshech* from above to below, the matter of the Ohr Ein Sof *lematah* until Ein Tachlis, there is also the matter of elevation from below to above; and in tefillah, which is an elevation from below to above, the matter of the Ohr Ein Sof *ma'alah* until Ein Ketz, there is also the matter of *hemshech* from above to below], it is revealed that the two matters [of *ma'alah* until Ein Ketz and *lematah* until Ein Tachlis] in their root are all one. As mentioned above (section 5), the reason for the inclusion of the two matters is that in the power of the *Atzmus*, the power to shine and the power not to shine are one matter. However, now, the revelation of the root of the two matters as they are in the power of the *Atzmus* is only in that they are included in one another, and this is a preface to the fact that in the time to come there will be the *connection and unification* of the two matters. For then it will shine within them in actual revelation, their first root as they are in the power of the *Atzmus*, where the power to shine and the power not to shine are truly one.

9) This is "the world stands on three things"—on three things the concealment stands. For through the Torah and mitzvos being divided into three categories, which generally are the two lines of *hemshech* and elevation, and even after they are divided into two lines they are included in one another, it is revealed that the inner essence of the world, concealment, is that there should be a revelation of Elokus within it. Therefore, the revelation of Elokus that is drawn into the world through the avodah in the three things—Torah, avodah, and gemilus chasadim (each of which is included in all of them)—is not like something added to the world, but rather that this is the essence of the world itself; the world stands.

This *hemshech* is through the avodah in the three things now. However, now it is in concealment, and in the time to come it will be in revelation, as explained above (in the previous maamar) that in the time of Moshiach there will be the connection of *ma'alah* and *matah*, and through it, this will be drawn down also into the entire world, that "the earth shall be full of the knowledge of Hashem", that even the physical earth (inanimate, *lematah matah*) will be full of the knowledge of Hashem (*ma'alah ma'alah*), with the coming of Moshiach Tzidkeinu, truly soon.

## Acharon Shel Pesach 5725

"And the spirit of Hashem shall rest upon him..." and "The wolf shall dwell with the lamb..." In his maamar titled *Zeh*, the Rebbe Rayatz explains that these verses describe the coming of Mashiach Tzidkeinu, who will come to redeem us speedily in our days. He states several things regarding Melech HaMashiach and his conduct. First, he explains the stature of Melech HaMashiach himself—"And the spirit of Hashem shall rest upon him," etc. Afterward, he explains his conduct—"And he shall be animated by the fear of Hashem," etc. (noting that "he shall be animated" [morach] also refers to his conduct, as he smells [morach] and judges). Following this, he adds, "The wolf shall dwell with the lamb," etc., meaning that the conduct of Mashiach will be such that he will effect a revelation of Elokus throughout the entire world—not only in the category of *medaber* (humanity), but even in the category of *chai* (the animal kingdom: wolf, leopard, etc.), and even in the category of *domem* (inanimate matter). As the verse continues, "For the earth shall be full of the knowledge of Hashem," meaning that even the physical earth (domem) will be filled with the knowledge of Hashem.

The maamar notes that one must understand: since the earth will then be full of the knowledge of Hashem, why will a king be necessary? The point of the explanation is that kingship is a matter of transcendence—the king is distinct and elevated above the people. Furthermore, the decrees of a king (even though, on the face of it, they are a movement of closeness, as the king draws near to the people to lead them according to his will) are in a manner of transcendence and distinction, by way of command and decree. The same is true from the perspective of the people: they fulfill the king’s command not because they know and grasp the reason for the command, but because of the fear and awe of the king—"that his awe may be upon you." Thus, the influence from the king to the people is also in a manner of transcendence and distinction. This is the difference between the influence of a *rav* and the influence of a king: the influence of a *rav* is that the *rav* draws near to the student (constricting his intellect to fit the student’s vessel) so that the student may grasp the intellect. The influence of a king, by contrast, is distinct from the people, above their intellect. The root of these two matters (the influence of a *rav* and a king) exists also in Torah, which contains (analogously) these two aspects. There are matters in Torah that are clothed in intellect, but the essence of Torah is above intellect. In the future to come, both aspects will be revealed. This is why Mashiach is called by the name *rav* and by the name *king*. That is to say, in addition to Mashiach teaching Torah to all of Yisrael and influencing the world with the taste and knowledge of the secrets and the inwardness of Torah—by virtue of which influence Mashiach is called *rav*—he himself will grasp infinitely more than what he influences into comprehension. The influence of these matters will be in a manner of *makif* (encompassing), analogous to the influence of a king. One might add, seemingly, that according to the known principle that the Torah study of Mashiach (what he will teach the entire people) will be in a manner of *re'iyah* (vision) that is above comprehension, it is understood from this that the matters he will influence in the manner of a king are such that they cannot come into revelation even through a vision that is above intellect. Nevertheless, because Mashiach will (also) be a king, he will reveal these matters to Yisrael as well, though the revelation of these matters will be in a manner of *makif*.

2) The maamar continues that all of this (both the influence of a *rav* and the influence of a king) comes specifically through the prerequisite of the *avodah* of the time of *galus*. To understand the connection between the *avodah* of the time of *galus* and the revelation of the future to come, the maamar introduces the premise that due to the magnitude and abundance of the concealments and hiddenness of the time of *galus*, the primary *avodah* during *galus* is by way of *nitzachon* (victory)—to be strong in the actual performance of *Torah u'Mitzvos*, without taking into account any preventer or obstacle.
